Post Name

Total Post

UPPSC APO Eligibility

Assistant Prosecution Officer APO 182
  • Bachelor Degree in Law (LLB) from Any Recognized University in India.
  • For Subject Wise Eligibility Details Read the Notification.

How to Fill UPPSC Assistant Prosecution Officer APO  Recruitment Online Form 2025

  • Uttar Pradesh Public Service Commission UPPSC Call For New Application for UPPSC Uttar Pradesh Assistant Prosecution Officer APO  Recruitment Examination 2025 Recruitment Candidate Can Apply Between 16/09/2025 to 16/10/2025
  • It is mandatory to have OTR i.e. One Time Registration for UPPSC Recruitment.
  • Pay attention, the registration number is received only after 72 hours of OTR registration - that's why OTR should be done first and only then the application will be made.
  • Candidate Read the Notification Before Apply the Recruitment Application Form in Uttar Pradesh UPPSC Latest Uttar Pradesh Assistant Prosecution Officer APO UP Government Jobs 2025.
  • Kindly Check and Collect the All Document - Eligibility, ID Proof, Address Details, Basic Details.
  • Kindly Ready Scan Document Related to Recruitment Form - Photo, Sign, ID Proof, Etc.
  • Before Submit the Application Form Must Check the Preview and All Column Carefully.
  • If Candidate Required to Paying the Application Fee Must Submit. If You have Not the Required Application Fees Your Form is Not Completed.
  • Take A Print Out of Final Submitted Form.

How to Fill : UPSSSC Sachiv Grade II Recruitment Online Form 2024

  • There are 02 types to apply online for UPSSSC Sachiv Grade II Recruitment Advt No. 06-Exam/2024 Recruitment 2024.
  • First: In this, the candidate will have to login with his personal information, the information that has to be given is: PET Registration Number, Date of Birth, Gender, Domicile and Category.
  • Second : In this, the candidate will have to login through his OTP, the information that has to be given is: UPSSSC PET 2023 Registration Number and OTP Password.
  • After login, the complete information of the candidate, his photo and signature will also be visible, the candidate will have to fill the information related to the post he is asking for and the application fee: Rs.25/- will have to be paid.
  • Kindly Check and Collect the All Document - Eligibility, ID Proof, Address Details, Basic Details.
  • Kindly Ready Scan Document Related to Recruitment Form - Photo, Sign, ID Proof, Etc.

Chhattisgarh Board CGBSE 10th & 12th Exam 2026

  • Chhattisgarh Board of Secondary Education conducted the class 10th examination 2026 in which the total registered students are 3 Lack+ and in class 12 the total registered students are 234352, who have been waiting for the result of the examination for a long time,  29/04/2026, their wait is going to end because the result will be released today at 02:30 pm on the official website of Chhattisgarh Board cgbse.nic.in and results.cg.nic.in, students can get the exam result direct link on our website https://www.OnlyJobs/.

Those Candidates Are Enrolled with CGBSE Board, Raipur Can Check the Result to Enter Your Seat / Roll No. to Download Result Link and View Your Result / Marks.

  • Minimum Age : 30Years
  • Maximum Age: 45Years

How to Fill UPPSC Assistant Registrar Online Form 2024

  • Uttar Pradesh Public Service Commission UPPSC Call For New Application for UPPSC Uttar Pradesh University Central Service Assistant Registrar Examination 2024 Recruitment 2023 Candidate Can Apply Between 28/08/2024 to 28/09/2024
  • It is mandatory to have OTR i.e. One Time Registration for UPPSC Recruitment.
  • Pay attention, the registration number is received only after 72 hours of OTR registration - that's why OTR should be done first and only then the application will be made.
  • Candidate Read the Notification Before Apply the Recruitment Application Form in Uttar Pradesh UPPSC Latest Uttar Pradesh Assistant Registrar Exam 2024 - UP Government Jobs 2024.
  • Kindly Check and Collect the All Document - Eligibility, ID Proof, Address Details, Basic Details.
  • Kindly Ready Scan Document Related to Recruitment Form - Photo, Sign, ID Proof, Etc.
  • Before Submit the Application Form Must Check the Preview and All Column Carefully.
  • If Candidate Required to Paying the Application Fee Must Submit. If You have Not the Required Application Fees Your Form is Not Completed.
  • Take A Print Out of Final Submitted Form.

Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE)

CBSE Board Class Xth and Class XIIth Result 2026

CBSE Results 2026 | Short Details of Notification

OnlyJobs® Official WWW.OnlyJobs

Important Dates

  • Class 10th High School Exam Between : 17/02/2026 to 11/03/2026
  • Class 12th Intermediate Exam Between : 17/02/2026 to 10/04/2026
  • Digilocker Account Activate Through Security Pin :14/04/2026
  • Class 10th Result Declared : 15/04/2026
  • Class 10th Second Board Exam Date : 15-21 May 2026
  • Class 12th Result Declared : April / May 2026

Application Fee

  • No Application Fee for the Checking CBSE Board Term II Class X & XII Exam Result & Activate Your DIGI Locker Account Through Security Pin.
 

OnlyJobs® :  How to Activate DIGILOCKER Account Through School Security Pin 2026

  • CBSE has issued a notice on 14 April 2026, in which it has been said that the annual board examination year 2026 of class 10 and 12 results / certificates and migration will be through Digilocker.
  • All CBSE Board Class 10 and 12 students who have also appeared in the exam will have to create their own account in Digilocker.
  • CBSE Board students can create their account in Digilocker by following some simple steps - which CBSE has also mentioned in its notice.
  • Once the account is created in Digilocker, candidates can also download their CBSE Board 2026 result, marksheet, certificate and migration online after the issue.
  • Step by Step to Activate CBSE Student to Activate Digilocker Account.
  • Step 1 : The student will get the link to do DigiLocker Account Confirmation in the Some Useful Important Links section at the bottom of this page.
  • Step 2 :To confirm DigiLocker Account, the student has to first select his class such as X or XII.
  • Step 3 : After that the student has to enter the code of his school. If the school code is not known, then they can see it in their admit card.
  • Step 4 : After that the student has to enter his/her board exam roll number.
  • Step 5 : After that Enter 6 Digit PIN provided by your school.
  • Step 6 : After that the candidate has to enter his mobile number.
  • Step 7 : An OTP will be received on the mobile number given by the student, he will have to enter it.
  • Step 8 : DigiLocker Account Confirmation process will be completed.
  • Now waiting for the candidate, whenever the CBSE Board will release the result of 2026 examination, candidates can also check their result through Digilocker as well as download the passing certificate / mark sheet and migration.

How to Check CBSE Board 10th & 12th Results 2026

  • CBSE Board Exam 2026 result can be released anytime in the month of April / May 2026.
  • After the release of CBSE result of board exam, candidates can check their result through two means.
  • After the release of CBSE result, candidates can check their class 10 and 12 result through CBSEResult and Digilocker whose direct link will be found in OnlyJobs Some Useful Important Link of this page.
  • The student must have his/her roll number to view the CBSE Result 2026.
  • All the registered students of CBSE Exam 2026 must activate their Digilocker account.

FOLLOW US

Are you waiting for RPSC Agriculture Officer Result 2026? Great news! The Rajasthan Public Service Commission has officially published the result for Agriculture Officer posts on 29th April 2026. Thousands of candidates who appeared in the examination held in October 2025 across various centres in Rajasthan can now verify their qualifying status online.

This article provides complete information about RPSC Agriculture Officer Result 2026 including direct download links, provisional list, cutoff marks, and step-by-step instructions to check results.

Check Your Result Now

How to Check RPSC Agriculture Officer Result 2026?

Candidates can check their RPSC Agriculture Officer Result 2026 by following this simple process:

  1. Visit the official website: https://rpsc.rajasthan.gov.in
  2. Go to the “Results” or “News and Events” section
  3. Click on the link for “Result Preamble and Cutoff Marks (Provisional List for Eligibility Checking) for Agriculture Officer”
  4. Download the PDF and search for your Roll Number
  5. Save the PDF for future reference

RPSC Agriculture Officer Merit List 2026 - What's Inside?

The RPSC Agriculture Officer merit list 2026 / provisional list contains details of candidates shortlisted for document verification / eligibility checking. RPSC releases category-wise cutoffs and lists.

Merit List / Result PDF Contains:

  • Roll Numbers of shortlisted candidates
  • Cutoff Marks (Category-wise)
  • Provisional List for Eligibility Checking
  • Instructions for next stage (Document Verification)

Types of Lists Released:

  1. Main List: Candidates qualified for eligibility checking
  2. Reserve List: Additional candidates (if applicable)
  3. Cutoff Marks: Minimum qualifying marks for different categories

Important Instructions for Qualified / Shortlisted Candidates

  • ✓ Download and save the result PDF immediately
  • ✓ Take printouts for future reference
  • ✓ Keep all original documents ready for verification
  • ✓ Check official website regularly for Document Verification schedule
  • ✓ Verify your details and report discrepancies if any
  • ✓ Prepare all required certificates as per RPSC guidelines

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 has been released by the Staff Selection Commission (SSC).

As per the latest updates, the final marks and scorecard were made available on February 17, 2026, at the official website ssc.gov.in. This follows the declaration of the SSC GD final result on January 15, 2026.

FOLLOW US

Candidates who appeared in the PST/PET and DV/DME/RME stages can now download their SSC GD final scorecard 2025 using their registration number and password. The scorecard displays normalized CBT marks and overall qualifying status after all recruitment stages. The download window is limited - typically a short period - so act quickly before the link deactivates.

Key Highlights:

  • Release Date: February 17, 2026
  • Last Date to Download: March 1, 2026 (till 6:00 PM)
  • Access: Online only via ssc.gov.in login
  • Purpose: View final marks, compare with cut-offs, and save for records/joining

SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 Overview

Particular Details
Exam Name Constable (GD) in CAPFs, SSF, NIA, Rifleman (GD) in Assam Rifles, and Sepoy in NCB Examination 2025
Conducting Body Staff Selection Commission (SSC)
Final Result Status Declared on January 15, 2026
Scorecard Status Released on February 17, 2026
Mode of Scorecard Online only
Login Credentials Registration number / Username and Password (plus captcha if required)
Official Website ssc.gov.in

Visit the Staff Selection Commission (SSC) official website – ssc.gov.in

Important Dates: SSC GD Final Result and Scorecard 2025

Event Date/Details
CBT Exam Dates February 4 to 25, 2025
PET/PST and Medical Stages Conducted post-CBT shortlisting
Final Result Declaration January 15, 2026
Final Scorecard Release February 17, 2026
Last Date to Download Scorecard March 1, 2026 (6:00 PM)

Direct Link to Check SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Candidates can access the SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 through the direct link on the SSC portal under the “Result/Marks” section after logging in. The portal may redirect from legacy links, but use ssc.gov.in primarily.

SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 – Direct Link (Active)
(Login required at ssc.gov.in for marks download)

Step-by-Step Guide to Download SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

  1. Visit the official Staff Selection Commission website: ssc.gov.in.
  2. Click on the “Login” or “Candidate Login” button on the homepage.
  3. Enter your registration number/username, password, and captcha (if prompted).
  4. After logging in, navigate to the “Result/Marks” tab on your dashboard.
  5. Select “Constable (GD) in CAPFs, SSF, NIA and Rifleman (GD) in Assam Rifles Examination 2025” from the list.
  6. Click on the “SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025” or “Marks” link to view your details.
  7. Your final scorecard will display on screen - review marks, cut-off comparison, and status.
  8. Download the PDF and print it for future reference (e.g., document verification). Save it promptly, as access ends after the deadline.
  9. (Use the direct link mentioned above to reach the login faster if available.)

Details Mentioned on SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Your scorecard typically includes:

  • Candidate’s name, roll number, registration number
  • Category/sub-category (UR, OBC, SC, ST, EWS, etc.)
  • Exam name and applied post
  • Section-wise marks (General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Knowledge & General Awareness, Elementary Mathematics, English/Hindi)
  • Raw and normalized marks
  • Total marks in CBT (considered for final merit after qualifying PET/PST and medical)
  • Qualifying status (selected/not selected) and any remarks
  • Note on limited availability window

SSC GD Final Cut Off and Merit List 2025

The SSC GD final cut off 2025 was released with the final result on January 15, 2026, and is force-wise, state-wise, category-wise, and gender-wise. It appears in the official result PDF write-up.

Your scorecard marks should be compared to the relevant cut-off for your category/force/state to assess selection. The merit list PDF lists roll numbers of provisionally selected candidates (around 50,047 selected against vacancies), while the scorecard provides individual marks for those who reached physical/medical stages.

(Refer to the official SSC result PDFs for exact cut-off tables - examples range from ~104 to 152+ depending on category/force.)

What to Do After Downloading SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Selected candidates: Keep multiple printouts/PDFs of the scorecard and final result for document verification, joining, and force allocation.

Verify details (name, roll number, category, etc.) - report serious discrepancies to SSC regional offices promptly.

Non-selected candidates: Use marks for performance analysis and preparation for future SSC exams.

Download and save copies early - the window closes after the last date.

SSC GD Selection Process 2025 (Short Overview)

The process included:

  • Computer-Based Test (CBT): Four sections totaling 160 marks.
  • Physical Efficiency Test (PET) and Physical Standard Test (PST): Qualifying.
  • Detailed Medical Examination (DME)/Review Medical Examination (RME): Qualifying.
  • Final merit based on normalized CBT marks (for qualified candidates), followed by force/state allocation per vacancies and preferences.

The final scorecard reflects CBT performance post-qualifying stages, available briefly post-result.

Important Links for SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

  • SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 Download: ssc.gov.in (login to Result/Marks)
  • SSC GD Final Result 2025 Merit List PDF: Available on ssc.gov.in under Results
  • SSC GD Final Cut Off 2025 PDF: In final result write-up on ssc.gov.in
  • SSC Official Website: ssc.gov.in

NEET UG Eligibility 2026

  • Passed 10+2 Intermediate Exam with Physics, Chemistry, Biology PCB Group of Subjects in Any Recognized Board in India.
  • For More Details Kindly See the Full Information Brochure or Read the Code Wise Eligibility Details.

NEET UG 2026 Age Limit

  • Minimum Age : 17 Years
  • Maximum Age : No Limit
  • born on or before 31.12.2009
  • Age Relaxation Extra as per Rules.
 

Eligibility Code for NTA NEET 2026 UG

  • NEET UG Code 01: A candidate who is appearing in the qualifying examination, i.e., 12 Standard in 2025 whose result is awaited may apply and take up the said test but he/she shall not be eligible for admission to the MBBS or BDS, if he/she does not pass the qualifying examination with the required pass percentage of marks at the time of first round of Counseling.
  • NEET UG Code 02 : The Higher/Senior Secondary Examination or the Indian School Certificate Examination which is equivalent to 10+2 Higher/Senior Secondary Examination after a period of 12 years study, the last two years of such study comprising of Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Bio-technology (which shall include practical tests in these subjects) and Mathematics or any other elective subject with English at a level not less than the core course for English as prescribed by the National Council of Educational Research and Training after introduction of the 10+2+3 educational structure as recommended by the National Committee on Education.
  • NEET UG Code 03 : The Intermediate/Pre-degree Examination in Science of an Indian University/Board or other recognized examining body with Physics, Chemistry, Biology /Bio-technology (which shall include practical test in these subjects) and also English as a compulsory subject
  • NEET UG Code 04 : The Pre-professional/Pre-medical Examination with Physics ,Chemistry, Biology/Bio-technology & English after passing either the Higher Secondary Examination or the Pre-University or an equivalent examination. The Pre-professional/Pre-medical examination shall include practical test in these subjects and also English as a compulsory subject.
  • NEET UG Code 05 : The first year of the three years’ degree course of a recognized University with Physics, Chemistry and Biology/Bio-technology including practical tests in these subjects provided the examination is a University Examination and candidate has passed the earlier qualifying examination with Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Bio-Technology with English at a level not less than a core course.
  • NEET UG Code 06 : B.Sc.Examination of an Indian University provided that he/she has passed the B.Sc.Examination with not less than two of the subjects Physics, Chemistry, Biology (Botany, Zoology)/Bio-technology and further that he/she has passed the earlier qualifying examination with Physics,Chemistry,Biology and English.
  • NEET UG Code 07: Any other examination which in scope and standard (Last 02 years of 10+2 Study
    comprising of Physics, Chemistry and Biology/Biotechnology; which shall include
    practical test in these subjects) is found to be equivalent to the Intermediate Science
    Examination of an Indian University/Board, taking Physics, Chemistry and
    Biology/Biotechnology including practical tests in each of these subjects and English.

Important Instruction for NEET UG

  • Aadhar Card is Compulsory in All India NTA NEET 2026 Examination
  • Instruction Of Photo  : Candidate Live Photo & Clear Passport Size and 4x6 Size Photo  (Visible Including Ears) with White
  • Background  and Also Enter Your Candidates Name and Photo Taken Date after 01/01/2026
  • For More Details Kindly  SEE NEET UG 2026 Notification

Short Information : Delhi Subordinate Service Selection Board (DSSSB) Are Recently Uploaded Exam Admit Card / Hall Ticket / Call Letter for the Various Recruitment 2017,2018,2019,2020,2021, 2022,2023, 2024, 2025, 2026. Those Candidates Are Enrolled with Any DSSSB Vacancies Can Download the Admit Card as per Schedule.


Delhi Subordinate Service Selection Board (DSSSB)

DSSSB Exam Admit Card 2026 | Exam Date for Various Post Recruitment

Short Details of Notification

WWW.OnlyJobs

Exam Conducted By

  • Delhi Subordinate Service Selection Board DSSSB Are Conducted the Various Recruitment Exam in Every Year.

Application Fee

  • No Application Fee to Download Admit Card / Hall Ticket.
  • If Objection in Question Paper (Answer Key) Required to Pay Objection Fees.

DSSSB How to Download Admit Card

  • It is very easy to download Delhi DSSSB Admit Card / Call Letter.
  • DSSSB issues the admit card in two main ways.
  • First Method: In this method, you will have to enter your date of birth along with class 10 roll number, passing year and your password, after that you can directly download the admit card from the dashboard.
  • Second Method : In this method, you will have to enter the application number of the online application you had applied, along with you will also have to enter your date of birth, after that you will be entered in a dashboard, then you will see a tab above, in that you will get the link of generate admit card, click on it. But your admit card will be downloaded on your mobile / computer.
  • If you do not have the application number then you have to follow the first method and find your application number.
  • You can print the admit card in color or black and white print on A4 size paper.
  • Read all the instructions given in the admit card carefully before going to the exam.

DSSSB How to View Question Paper and Download Answer Key and

  • You have to click on the link given below to view and download the answer key and question paper.
  • To login, you have to enter your date of birth along with the application number, after that you can see the answer given by you, you can also get information about the correct answer, if you have an objection in any answer, then you can do from the same.

Enrolled Candidate Can Download the Admit Card.

UPSSSC Junior Engineer Mains Admit Card 2026 OUT - Download Hall Ticket at upsssc.gov.in

UPSSSC Junior Engineer Mains Admit Card 2026 has been released by Uttar Pradesh Subordinate Services Selection Commission on 29 April 2026. Candidates who applied for the Junior Engineer (Civil) recruitment under Advt. No. 08-Exam/2024 and completed the mains exam fee payment can download their admit card from the official website upsssc.gov.in. The written mains examination is scheduled for 03 May 2026 (Sunday) from 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM across various centers in Uttar Pradesh. This hall ticket is mandatory for appearing in the exam. Read below for the direct download link, step-by-step process, exam details, and important instructions for UPSSSC JE Mains Admit Card 2026.

How to Download UPSSSC Junior Engineer Mains Admit Card 2026?

Follow these simple steps to download your UPSSSC JE Mains Admit Card 2026:

  1. Step 1: Visit the official UPSSSC website at upsssc.gov.in or click the direct download link provided above
  2. Step 2: Navigate to the "Admit Card" or "Download Admit Card" section on the homepage
  3. Step 3: Click on "UPSSSC Junior Engineer Mains Admit Card 2026" link
  4. Step 4: Enter your credentials:
    • Registration Number / Application Number
    • Date of Birth (DD/MM/YYYY format)
    • Gender (if required)
    • Captcha Code
  5. Step 5: Click on "Submit" or "Download Admit Card" button
  6. Step 6: Your UPSSSC Junior Engineer Mains Admit Card 2026 will appear on screen
  7. Step 7: Download and save the PDF file on your device
  8. Step 8: Take 2-3 printouts for exam day (keep backup copies)

Pro Tip: Check all details on your admit card immediately after download. Report any discrepancies to UPSSSC helpdesk without delay.

Details on UPSSSC Junior Engineer Mains Admit Card 2026

Your UPSSSC JE Mains Admit Card 2026 will contain the following information:

Candidate Information:

  • Candidate Name
  • Father's Name
  • Date of Birth
  • Category (General/OBC/SC/ST/EWS)
  • Gender
  • Registration/Roll Number
  • Candidate's Photograph and Signature

Exam Information:

  • Exam Name: Junior Engineer (Civil) Mains Exam
  • Exam Date: 03 May 2026
  • Exam Shift/Timing: 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM
  • Reporting Time
  • Exam Center Name and Complete Address
  • Exam Center Code

Important Instructions:

  • Documents to carry on exam day
  • Items allowed and not allowed in examination hall
  • Exam day guidelines and rules

Documents Required on UPSSSC Junior Engineer Exam Day

Mandatory Documents (Originals):

  • UPSSSC JE Mains Admit Card 2026 (printed copy with clear photograph)
  • Valid Photo ID Proof (any one of the following):
    • Aadhaar Card
    • Voter ID Card
    • Driving License
    • PAN Card
    • Passport
  • Recent passport-size photographs (2 copies - same as uploaded in application)
  • Simple pen (blue/black ballpoint)

Optional but Recommended:

  • Pencil and eraser (for rough work)
  • Water bottle (transparent, without label)

Not Allowed in Examination Hall:

  • Mobile phones and smartphones
  • Smartwatches and digital watches
  • Calculators and electronic devices
  • Books, notes, and study materials
  • Bags and pouches

Important Instructions for UPSSSC Junior Engineer Mains Exam 2026

Before Exam Day:

  • Download admit card immediately and verify all details
  • Check exam center location on Google Maps
  • Plan your route and transportation in advance
  • Arrange all required documents
  • Keep backup copies of admit card and ID proof

On Exam Day:

  • Reach exam center at least 60 minutes before reporting time
  • Entry closes before exam starts (No late entry allowed)
  • Follow all instructions mentioned on the admit card
  • Keep calm and focused

What If UPSSSC Junior Engineer Mains Admit Card Not Available?

If you're unable to download your UPSSSC JE Mains Admit Card 2026, try these solutions:

  1. Solution 1: Clear browser cache and cookies, then try again
  2. Solution 2: Try different web browser (Chrome recommended)
  3. Solution 3: Verify that you have paid the mains exam fee successfully
  4. Solution 4: Contact UPSSSC helpdesk immediately

Important: Act immediately if facing issues. Don't wait until exam day.

Stay Updated: Bookmark this page or official website for latest updates on UPSSSC JE Mains Result 2026, answer key, and all recruitment notifications.

SSC GD Constable Admit Card 2026 OUT - Download Hall Ticket at ssc.gov.in

SSC GD Constable Admit Card 2026 has been released by Staff Selection Commission on 25 April 2026. Candidates who applied for the Constable (GD) in CAPFs, SSF, Rifleman (GD) in Assam Rifles and Sepoy in NCB recruitment can download their admit card from the official website ssc.gov.in. The Computer Based Test (CBT) is scheduled from 27 April 2026 to 30 May 2026 in multiple phases across various centers. This hall ticket is mandatory for appearing in the exam. Read below for the direct download link, step-by-step process, exam details, and important instructions for SSC GD Constable Admit Card 2026.

FOLLOW US

How to Download SSC GD Constable Admit Card 2026?

Follow these simple steps to download your SSC GD Constable Admit Card 2026:

  1. Step 1: Visit the official SSC website at ssc.gov.in or click the direct download link provided above
  2. Step 2: Click on the "Admit Card" or "Candidate Login" section
  3. Step 3: Select the appropriate region and click on "SSC GD Constable Admit Card 2026" link
  4. Step 4: Enter your credentials:
    • Registration Number / Roll Number
    • Password / Date of Birth
    • Captcha Code
  5. Step 5: Click on "Submit" or "Download Admit Card" button
  6. Step 6: Your SSC GD Constable Admit Card 2026 will appear on screen
  7. Step 7: Download and save the PDF file on your device
  8. Step 8: Take 2-3 printouts for exam day (keep backup copies)

Pro Tip: Check all details on your admit card immediately after download. Report any discrepancies to SSC regional office within 24 hours. City Intimation Slip was released earlier on 19 April 2026.

Details on SSC GD Constable Admit Card 2026

Your SSC GD Constable Admit Card 2026 will contain the following information:

Candidate Information:

  • Candidate Name
  • Roll Number / Registration Number
  • Date of Birth
  • Category
  • Gender
  • Photograph and Signature

Exam Information:

  • Exam Name: SSC GD Constable CBT 2026
  • Exam Date & Shift
  • Reporting Time
  • Exam Center Name and Complete Address
  • Exam Center Code

Important Instructions:

  • Documents to carry on exam day
  • Items allowed and not allowed in examination hall
  • Exam day rules and guidelines

Documents Required on SSC GD Constable Exam Day

Mandatory Documents (Originals):

  • SSC GD Constable Admit Card 2026 (printed copy with clear photograph)
  • Valid Photo ID Proof (any one of the following):
    • Aadhaar Card
    • Voter ID Card
    • Driving License
    • PAN Card
    • Passport
  • Recent passport-size photographs (if required)

Not Allowed in Examination Hall:

  • Mobile phones and electronic devices
  • Books, notes, or study materials
  • Calculators or smartwatches

Important Instructions for SSC GD Constable Exam 2026

Before Exam Day:

  • Download admit card as soon as available
  • Verify exam center location
  • Plan your travel in advance
  • Keep backup copies ready

On Exam Day:

  • Reach exam center at least 60 minutes before reporting time
  • No entry after gate closure
  • Follow all COVID or security guidelines if mentioned

During Exam:

  • Follow invigilator instructions
  • Manage time effectively
  • Do not carry prohibited items

What If SSC GD Constable Admit Card Not Available?

If you're unable to download your SSC GD Constable Admit Card 2026, try these solutions:

  1. Clear browser cache and cookies, then retry
  2. Use a different browser (Chrome recommended)
  3. Check your application status on ssc.gov.in
  4. Contact your regional SSC office or helpdesk immediately

Important: Resolve any issues well before your exam date. Admit cards are released region-wise and a few days prior to your specific shift.

Not Allowed in Examination Hall:

Important Instructions for RSSB Agriculture Supervisor Exam 2026

  • Don't panic if questions seem difficult

What If RSSB Agriculture Supervisor Admit Card Not Available?

If you're unable to download your RSSB Agriculture Supervisor Admit Card 2026, try these solutions:

Important: Act immediately if facing issues downloading admit card. Don't wait until exam day. Contact RSSB helpdesk at least 2 days before the exam.

SSC One Time Registration OTR Registration 2025

SSC OTR 2025 | Short Details of Notification

WWW.OnlyJobs

Important Dates

  • Aadhar Enable OTR Application Begin : 02/06/2025
  • Last Date for Registration : NA

Application Fee

  • General / OBC / EWS : 0/-
  • SC / ST : 0/-
  • No Application Fees

SSC OTR Eligibility

  • One time registration is necessary only for those who want to appear in any SSC recruitment in future. With the help of this registration, the candidate will save a lot of time while applying.
  • No Age Limit in SSC OTR Registration.

SSC Popular Vacancy 

  • SSC Combined Graduate Level CGL Exam
  • SSC Multi Tasking Staff MTS Exam
  • SSC Selection Post Exam
  • SSC Constable GD Recruitment Exam
  • SSC Delhi Police Constable Recruitment Exam
  • SSC Delhi Police Sub Inspector Exam
  • SSC CPO SI Recruitment Exam
  • SSC CHSL 10+2 Recruitment Exam
  • SSC Stenographer Exam
  • SSC Junior Engineer JE Exam
  • SSC Junior Hindi Translator JHT Exam

How to Apply SSC One Time Registration OTR Online Form 2025

  • Staff Selection Commission SSC issued a notice on 22 February 2024 saying that they have launched a new website SSC Gov in, in which all the candidates will have to do fresh one time registration (OTR)
  • Step 1 : Open the Website ssc.gov.in or Direct Link Will be in this Page Some Useful Important Links Section
  • Step 2 : Click on New Registration Tab
  • Step 3 : Students will have to give their basic information such as: Name, Father's Name, Mother's Name, Mobile Number, Email Id and High School Class 10 Roll Number.
  • Step 4 : Verify Your OTR Profile Details & Submit Registration.
  • Step 5 : An OTP will be received on the registered email and registered mobile number of the candidate, he will have to enter it.
  • Step 6 : The registration Part I process will be completed, the candidate will see the OTR Registration No. on the screen.
  • Step 7 : In the next step, you will have to change the default password which has been received on your email, after that you will have to login by writing the registration number along with the password which has been received on the email.
  • Step 9 : Candidates should change the default password of OTR registration number with their custom password. Use capital letters, small letters, numbers and special characters while creating the new password which should be at least 08 digits. Additionally, two security questions will also have to be selected, so that if you forget your password in future, you can recover your password with the help of the security question.
  • Step 10 :  After changing the password, the candidate will have to login again, after that the information about his nationality, category, Visible Identification Mark and information about permanent and local address will be entered in the form.
  • Step 11 : The last step is that here you have to first double check all the information by clicking on the preview button of OTR, after checking, if there is any error then correct it, otherwise click on the declare button and submit the form finally.
  • Step 12 : The registration process of SSC OTR has now been completed, the candidate will be able to see the dashboard whenever any new recruitment comes, or the candidate can also get the information about admit card and result by logging into the SSC dashboard.
  • Our team has also shown a video on how to do SSC one time registration, which you can watch and do your new registration by following the step by step process: SSC OTR Registration Kaise Kare Video Dekhe
  • For more information, you have to complete the notice issued to SSC OTR.

Application Fee  

  • General/OBC/EWS : 2000/-
  • SC / ST / PH : 1000/-
  • Pay the Exam Fee Through Online Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking  or Pay the Exam Fee Through Offline Mode Only.

UPHESC Assistant Professor Notification 2025 : Age Limit Details

  • Minimum Age : NA
  • Maximum Age : 62 Years.
  • Age Relaxation Extra as per UPHESC Assistant Professor Recruitment Rules.

UPESSC Assistant Professor 2025 :  Vacancy Details Total : 107 Post

Post Name

Total Post

UPESSC Assistant Professor Eligibility

Assistant Professor Bed

107
  • Master Degree in Science / Mathematics / Social Science / Commerce / Language (BED Subjects) with Minimum 55% Marks,
  • M.Ed Exam with 55% Marks OR
  • Master Degree in Sociology with 55% Marks with B.Ed / BLEd / Equivalent Degree.
  • UGC NET / PHd / D.Phill
  • More Details Read the Notification

How to Fill UPHESC Assistant Professor Online Form 2026

  • Note : Applicants who have already applied under the B.Ed subject need to apply again.
  • Uttar Pradesh Education Service Selection Commission UPESSC, Uttar Pradesh Higher Education Service Commission UPHESC 51 Are Issued Assistant Professor BEd Subject Recruitment 2025 – Advt No. 51. Candidate Can Re Apply Apply Between 28/04/2026 to 27/05/2026.
  • Candidate Read the Notification Before Apply the Recruitment Application Form in UPESSC Advt No. 51 Assistant Professor BEd Subject Recruitment 2025.
  • Kindly Check and Collect the All Document - Eligibility, ID Proof, Address Details, Basic Details.
  • Kindly Ready Scan Document Related to Recruitment Form - Photo, Sign, ID Proof, Etc.
  • Before Submit the Application Form Must Check the Preview and All Column Carefully.
  • Take A Print Out of Final Submitted Form.

Interested Candidates Can Read the Full Notification Before Apply Online.

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026: Get the latest RBI recruitment notification 2026 for 60 Officer in Grade 'B' (DR) vacancies under the Reserve Bank of India Services Board (General/DEPR/DSIM Cadres) - Panel Year 2026. The online application process starts on 29th April 2026 and the last date to apply online is 20th May 2026. Find RBI vacancies across India on the official website rbi.org.in. You can also get OnlyJobs of "Upcoming RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026" notifications for freshers and experienced candidates. Also know the direct official link for RBI Grade B Recruitment 2026 apply online from the RBI career page.

FOLLOW US

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The Reserve Bank of India Services Board has officially released Advertisement No. RBISB/DA/01/2026-27 for recruitment to 60 posts of Officers in Grade 'B' (Direct Recruit-DR) (On Probation-OP) under the General, DEPR, and DSIM Cadres – Panel Year 2026. The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility, selection process, examination syllabus, PwBD category classification, fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to download and read the full notification and its annexures before applying online. The notification PDF and syllabus are available on the official RBI website.

  • Download RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 Overview

Field Details
Commission/Organization Name Reserve Bank of India Services Board (RBISB), Mumbai
Advertisement No. RBISB/DA/01/2026-27
Post Name Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) (On Probation) – General / DEPR / DSIM Cadres
Department Reserve Bank of India (RBI)
Service General Cadre / DEPR Cadre / DSIM Cadre
Total Vacancies 60 (including PwBD posts)
Pay Scale Starting Basic Pay ₹78,450/- p.m. in the pay scale of ₹78450-4050(9)-114900-EB-4050(2)-123000-4650(4)-141600 (16 years); Gross emoluments approx. ₹1,54,936/- p.m. (without HRA)
Application Mode Online only (via RBI website www.rbi.org.in)
Selection Mode Phase-I Online Examination + Phase-II Online/Written Examination + Interview
Mandatory Requirement Eligibility conditions as on April 01, 2026; valid nationality certificate where applicable
Official Website https://www.rbi.org.in

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Advertisement 29/04/2026
Opening of Online Application Portal 29-04-2026
Last Date for Online Application Submission 20-05-2026 (till 06:00 PM)
Correction Window (Edit Window) 2 days from the closing date of application submission
Age Calculated As On 01-04-2026
Phase-I Exam – General Cadre 13-06-2026
Phase-I Exam – DEPR / DSIM Cadre 14-06-2026
Phase-II Exam – General Cadre 25-07-2026
Phase-II Exam – DEPR / DSIM Cadre 26-07-2026

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 60 vacancies have been announced across three cadres – General, DEPR, and DSIM. Vacancies are distributed across GEN/UR, EWS, OBC, SC, and ST categories.

Post Total
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – General Cadre 40
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – DEPR Cadre 10
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – DSIM Cadre 10
Total 60

PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) Sub-Category Vacancy

Sr. No. Post PwBD Category A PwBD Category B PwBD Category C PwBD Category D
1 Grade 'B' (DR) – General – – 1 1
2 Grade 'B' (DR) – DEPR – – – –
3 Grade 'B' (DR) – DSIM 1 – – –

Category A: Blindness & Low Vision; B: Deaf & Hard of Hearing; C: Locomotor disability; D: Autism, intellectual disability, specific learning disability and mental illness

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on April 01, 2026. Nationality conditions apply – the post is open to citizens of India and certain other categories as specified in the notification, subject to eligibility certificate from the Government of India where required.

The educational qualification must be from a University incorporated by an Act of the Central or State Legislature, or an institution declared Deemed University under UGC Act, 1956, or an equivalent foreign university recognized by the Association of Indian Universities.

Educational Qualification

Post Minimum Educational Qualifications (as on April 01, 2026)
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – General Graduation in any discipline with minimum 60% marks (50% for SC/ST/PwBD if posts reserved for them) OR Post-Graduation with minimum 55% marks (pass marks for SC/ST/PwBD if posts reserved for them) in aggregate of all semesters/years.
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – DEPR Essential: MA/MSc in Economics or related Economics/Finance disciplines with minimum 55% marks (50% for SC/ST/PwBD if posts reserved) from a recognized Indian or Foreign University. Desirable: Doctorate in Economics, or research/teaching experience, or publication in standard journals.
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – DSIM Essential: Master's Degree with minimum 55% marks in Statistics/Mathematics/Data Science/AI/ML or related fields OR Four-year Bachelor's Degree with minimum 60% marks in the above fields (50% for SC/ST/PwBD if posts reserved). Desirable: Doctorate in related subjects; research/teaching experience and publications.

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must have attained the age of 21 years and must not have attained the age of 30 years as on April 01, 2026 (born not earlier than April 02, 1996 and not later than April 01, 2005). The upper age limit is relaxed for various categories as detailed below.

General/EWS candidates who have already appeared six times for Phase-I Examination for this post in the past are not eligible to apply. No such restriction applies to SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates if posts are reserved for them.

Category Age Limit
General (UR) / EWS 21 to 30 years (as on 01-04-2026)
SC / ST 5 years relaxation (max 35 years)
OBC 3 years relaxation (max 33 years)
PwBD – Gen/EWS 10 years relaxation (max 40 years)
PwBD – OBC 13 years relaxation (max 43 years)
PwBD – SC/ST 15 years relaxation (max 45 years)
Ex-Servicemen / Ex-Bank Employees 5 years relaxation
Candidates with M.Phil. Upper age limit 32 years
Candidates with Ph.D. Upper age limit 34 years
Bank Officers (experience-based relaxation) Up to 3 years relaxation; upper age not to exceed 33 years including experience
DEPR/DSIM – Master's with Research/Teaching Experience Up to 3 years relaxation

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates applying for the post of Officers in Grade 'B' are required to pay a non-refundable fee/intimation charges as specified below. The fee must be paid online through the RBI application portal. Fee paid will not be refunded under any circumstances nor held in reserve for any other examination or selection.

Candidates submitting applications without the prescribed fee/intimation charges will be summarily rejected. Candidates are therefore advised to confirm their eligibility and verify all details before making payment.

Category Charges Amount
SC / ST / PwBD Intimation Charges only ₹100/- + 18% GST
GEN / OBC / EWS Application Fee including Intimation Charges ₹850/- + 18% GST
RBI Staff (eligible Staff Candidates) Nil Nil

Bank/Transaction charges are to be borne by the candidate.

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for RBI Grade B Recruitment 2026 consists of Phase-I Online Examination, Phase-II Online/Written Examination, and Interview. Candidates who qualify Phase-I will be shortlisted for Phase-II based on aggregate marks. Only those who clear Phase-II will be called for the Interview.

No candidate will be called for the Interview unless he/she appears in Phase-II examination. The final merit list will be prepared by adding marks secured in Phase-II and Interview (and grace marks as applicable). If a candidate remains absent in the Interview, his/her candidature will not be considered for final selection.

The selection list recommended by the Board remains valid for the period as decided by the Board/Bank.

Stage 1 – Phase-I Online Examination (Grade 'B' DR – General)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Single Paper General Awareness, English Language, Quantitative Aptitude, Reasoning Objective MCQ 200 120 Minutes

Stage 1 – Phase-I Online/Written Examination (DEPR Cadre)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-I Economics – Objective Type Online MCQ 100 120 Minutes
Paper-II English – Descriptive Typed on keyboard 100 120 Minutes

Stage 1 – Phase-I Online Examination (DSIM Cadre)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-I Statistics – Objective Type Online MCQ 100 120 Minutes

Stage 2 – Phase-II Online Examination (Grade 'B' DR – General)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-I Economic and Social Issues 50% Objective + 50% Descriptive 100 120 Minutes
Paper-II English (Writing Skills) Descriptive (typed) 100 90 Minutes
Paper-III General Finance and Management 50% Objective + 50% Descriptive 100 120 Minutes
Grand Total     300  

Stage 2 – Phase-II Written Examination (DEPR Cadre)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-I Economics – Descriptive Pen/Paper (questions on screen) 100 120 Minutes
Paper-II Economics – Descriptive Pen/Paper (questions on screen) 100 120 Minutes
Total     400 (incl. Phase-I)  

Stage 2 – Phase-II Written Examination (DSIM Cadre)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-II Statistics – Descriptive Pen/Paper (questions on screen) 100 180 Minutes
Paper-III English – Descriptive Typed on keyboard 100 90 Minutes
Total     300 (incl. Phase-I)  

Stage 3 – Interview: 75 Marks (All Cadres)

Candidates will also undergo a Personality Assessment before the Interview. No marks are allotted for Personality Assessment and it does not form part of the final selection criteria. Candidates may opt for Interview in Hindi or English.

Qualifying Marks

Category Minimum Qualifying Marks
All Categories Minimum marks separately for each test/paper as prescribed by the Board; minimum aggregate cut-off for Phase-II shortlisting decided by the Board in relation to number of vacancies

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Exam Syllabus

Phase-I – General Cadre (200 Marks, Objective MCQ, 120 Minutes)

The paper covers General Awareness, English Language, Quantitative Aptitude, and Reasoning. Separate time will be allotted for each section. Detailed information will be provided in the Information Handout available with the Admit Card.

Phase-II – General Cadre

Paper-I (Economic and Social Issues): Growth and Development (National Income, poverty alleviation, sustainable development); Indian Economy (economic history, monetary and fiscal policy since 1991, Indian money and financial markets, public finance, agriculture, services sector); Globalization (BoP, export-import policy, IMF, World Bank, WTO); Social Structure in India (multiculturalism, demographic trends, urbanization, gender issues, social justice).

Paper-II (English – Writing Skills): Writing skills including expression and understanding of the topic.

Paper-III (General Finance and Management): Financial system (structure of financial institutions, RBI functions, banking system, digital payments); Financial markets (primary and secondary markets); General topics (financial risk management, derivatives, financial inclusion, corporate governance, Union Budget, accounting basics, inflation); Management (fundamentals, organizational behaviour, motivation, leadership, ethics, corporate governance, communication).

Phase-I – DEPR Cadre (Paper-I, Objective – Economics)

Microeconomics, Macroeconomics (IS-LM Model, schools of economic thought), International Economics, Theories of Economic Growth and Development, Public Finance, Environmental Economics, Quantitative Methods in Economics, Current developments in Indian Economy.

Phase-II – DEPR Cadre (Descriptive – Economics)

Paper-I covers Microeconomics Module (consumer theory, production theory, market theory, welfare economics) and Macroeconomics Module (national income accounting, employment and output, inflation, money and banking, growth theories, international trade, public finance) with equal weightage. Paper-II covers Quantitative Methods in Economics (mathematical methods, statistical methods, econometrics) and Indian Economy – Policy and Trends (fiscal policy, monetary policy, banking sector, inflation, external sector, sectoral developments) with equal weightage.

Phase-I – DSIM Cadre (Paper-I, Objective – Statistics) and Phase-II (Paper-II, Descriptive – Statistics)

Topics covered across both papers: Theory of Probability, Probability Distributions and Sampling Theory; Linear Models and Economic Statistics; Statistical Inference (Estimation, Testing of Hypothesis, Non-Parametric Tests); Stochastic Processes; Multivariate Analysis; Econometrics and Time Series; Optimization and Statistical Computing; Data Science, Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Techniques; Database and Data Warehouse Management.

How to Apply for RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the Bank's website www.rbi.org.in. No other means/mode of application will be accepted. The online portal will be active from 29-04-2026 to 20-05-2026 (6:00 PM). Candidates desirous of applying for both Grade 'B' (DR)-General and Grade 'B' (DR)-DEPR or DSIM must apply using separate links and pay fees for both. Digi Locker has been integrated with the online application form for voluntary submission of credentials.

Candidates should avoid submitting multiple applications; if done unavoidably, only the application with the higher Registration ID (RID) will be considered valid, and fee paid against other RIDs will not be adjusted. After the closing date, a 2-day edit window will be available for making corrections in limited fields. Candidates are not required to upload supporting documents at the time of online application; documents will be verified at the Interview stage only.

Documents Required at Interview Stage:

  • Age proof (birth certificate or Class X marksheet/certificate)
  • Educational qualification certificates and marksheets
  • SC / ST / OBC / PwBD / EWS / Ex-SM certificate (if applicable) in prescribed format
  • Photo identity proof (PAN Card / Passport / Driving Licence / Voter's Card / Aadhaar, etc.)
  • Online application form printout with fee payment receipt

Candidates working in Government/PSU/Public Enterprises must inform their employer in writing before applying and submit an undertaking to this effect at the time of online application. At the time of joining, proper discharge certificates from their employer must be produced.

Apply Online at: https://www.rbi.org.in

Important Instructions for RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Only eligible citizens (as defined in the notification) are eligible to apply; nationality certificate required for certain categories.
  • Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on April 01, 2026.
  • No hard copy applications will be accepted – online submission only.
  • Recruitment fee/intimation charges are non-refundable; applications without prescribed fee will be summarily rejected.
  • Government/PSU employees must submit an undertaking about informing their employer and bring discharge certificate at the time of joining.
  • Mobile phones, pagers, and all electronic devices are banned inside examination/interview premises; physical calculators are not permitted in any phase.
  • Candidates reporting after Gate Closure Time specified on the Admit Card will not be permitted to take the examination.
  • Candidates are advised to visit https://www.rbi.org.in regularly for updates; all corrigenda will be published on the Bank's website only.
  • Phase-I paper (General Cadre) will be set in English; all other papers (except English papers) will be set bilingually in Hindi and English.
  • Negative marking on MCQ questions will apply as prescribed; detailed marking scheme will be in the Information Handout.
  • For any problem in filling the form or downloading Admit Card, queries may be raised through http://cgrs.ibps.in mentioning the post and cadre in the subject line.
  • All decisions of the Board regarding eligibility, conduct of examinations, selection, and other matters are final and binding; any resultant dispute is subject to sole jurisdiction of Courts at Mumbai.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has published the Stenographer Grade ‘C’ & ‘D’ Examination 2026 Notification on 24th April 2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill approximately 731 tentative vacancies for Stenographer Grade C (Group B Non-Gazetted) & Grade D (Group C) posts in various Ministries/Departments/Organizations of Govt. of India. Candidates aged 18-30 years (Grade C) / 18-27 years (Grade D) as on 01.08.2026, with 12th standard pass & stenography skills, are eligible to apply online only at ssc.gov.in from 24 April to 15 May 2026

FOLLOW US

SSC Stenographer Grade C & D Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Staff Selection Commission (SSC), Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances & Pensions, Govt. of India has released detailed advertisement (F. No. E1/32/026-C-2) for Stenographer Grade ‘C’ & ‘D’ posts across Ministries/Departments/Organizations including Attached/Subordinate offices & Statutory bodies. Download the official detailed notification from SSC website to check tentative vacancies (to be updated), eligibility, skill test standards, fee & application process before applying

  • Download SSC Stenographer 2026 Detailed Advertisement PDF

Stenographer Grade D (Group ‘C’)

Application Mode Online only

SSC Stenographer Grade C & D Vacancy 2026

Approx. 731 tentative vacancies announced for Stenographer Grade C & D (exact post/category-wise to be updated on ssc.gov.in; State/Zone-wise vacancies not collected by SSC). Vacancies for ESM reserved only for Group C (Grade D). Updated vacancy details will be available on SSC website. Final vacancies: 232 Grade C + 1483 Grade D = 1715 (per some sources, subject to confirmation).

Note: No region-wise table provided in notification; check ssc.gov.in for updates.

SSC Stenographer Grade C & D Eligibility Criteria 2026

  • Nationality: Indian citizen / Subject of Nepal/Bhutan / Tibetan refugee (before 01.01.1962) / Person of Indian origin from specified countries (eligibility certificate required).

Essential Educational Qualification (as on 01.08.2026)

Post Name Educational Qualification
Stenographer Grade C & D 12th Standard or equivalent from recognized Board/University. Stenography skill mandatory (tested in Skill Test). 

SSC Stenographer Grade C & D Age Limit 2026

Crucial date: 01.08.2026 (born not before 02.08.1996 & not later than 01.08.2008 for Grade C; adjust for Grade D).

Category Age Limit (Grade C) Age Limit (Grade D)
Category Age Limit (Grade C) Age Limit (Grade D)
Minimum Age 18 years 18 years

OBC (NCL) 3 years relaxation 3 years relaxation

PwBD (UR/EWS) 10 years relaxation 10 years relaxation
PwBD (OBC-NCL) 13 years relaxation 13 years relaxation
PwBD (SC/ST) 15 years relaxation 15 years relaxation
Ex-Servicemen (ESM) 3 years after military service deduction (Group C only) 3 years after military service deduction
Central Govt. Civilian (3+ yrs service) Up to 40/45 (SC/ST) years Up to 40/45 (SC/ST) years 

SSC Stenographer Grade C & D Application Fee 2026

  • For General / OBC (NCL) / EWS: ₹100/-

  • For SC / ST / PwBD / ESM / Women: No fee (exempted)

  • Pay online via BHIM UPI / Net Banking / Card

SSC Stenographer Grade C & D Selection Process 2026

  • Computer Based Test (CBT): 200 marks (objective; General Intelligence & Reasoning 50, GA 50, English 100); qualifying: 30% UR, 25% OBC/EWS, 20% others.

  • Skill Test in Stenography (Qualifying): Grade C: 100 wpm (English/Hindi); Grade D: 80 wpm (English/Hindi) (10 min dictation; 40% UR/EWS, 35% others pass marks).

  • Document Verification (DV) by User Departments.

  • Final Merit: Based on CBT marks (category-wise); All India Service Liability.

Pre-Employment Medical Exam (esp. for BRO posts with Physical Efficiency Test).

SSC Stenographer 2026 Documents Required

Candidates must upload/produce originals at DV:

  • Recent passport size photo & signature.

  • Matric certificate (DOB proof).

  • 12th mark sheet & certificate.

  • Caste/EWS/PwBD/ESM certificate (GOI format).

  • NOC (Govt./PSU employees).

  • ID proof (Aadhaar/Voter ID etc.).

  • Others (name change, scribe cert. if applicable).

How to Apply for SSC Stenographer 2026

  • Apply online only via ssc.gov.in → One Time Registration (OTR) → Stenographer 2026 section.

  • Capture live photo (webcam), upload signature (10-20 KB JPEG).

  • Fill details, pay fee (if applicable), submit.

  • Correction window: 20-21 May 2026 (charges apply).

  • Print application for records; active email/mobile required.

  • Last date: 15 May 2026 (23:59 Hrs)

SSC Stenographer Grade C & D Online Application Form

Eligible candidates apply online through SSC career portal from 24 April 2026 (evening). Complete OTR first, then application with photo/signature upload. Helpline: 1800-309-3063.

Click Here to Apply Online for SSC Stenographer 2026

SSC Stenographer Grade C & D Remuneration 2026

Pay Component Details
Stenographer Grade C (post selection) Pay Level-7 (₹44,900 - 1,42,400); approx. CTC ₹10-12 LPA (incl. DA, HRA, perks).
Stenographer Grade D (post selection) Pay Level-4 (₹25,500 - 81,100); approx. CTC ₹6-8 LPA (incl. DA, HRA, perks). 
Additional Benefits DA, HRA/quarters, TA, medical, PF, gratuity, pension, leave encashment etc. as per rules.

Important Instructions for Candidates

  • Read notification carefully before applying.

  • Ensure eligibility (esp. stenography skill, age/EQ cut-off 01.08.2026).

  • Upload clear photo/signature; Aadhaar authentication recommended.

  • Govt/PSU: NOC/undertaking required.

  • Keep email/mobile active; no fee refund.

  • SSC reserves right to modify/cancel; no waiting list (sliding mechanism for FRTA).

  • Posts AISL; BRO posts need physical/medical standards (males only for BRO Grade D).

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has released the Selection Post Phase 14 Recruitment 2026 notification on 13 April 2026 for 2919 (Revised) vacancies across various Ministries, Departments and Offices of the Government of India and their Attached/Subordinate offices. Candidates with Matriculation (10th), Higher Secondary (10+2) and Graduation & above qualifications can apply online for various Selection Posts through the SSC website ssc.gov.in from 13 April 2026 to 04 May 2026.

FOLLOW US

SSC has updated the Selection Post Phase 14 vacancies by removing GSI posts and adding DRDO vacancies. Candidates can check the complete notification along with detailed post-wise information and codes. These posts are placed in Pay Levels 1 to 8 of the 7th CPC Pay Matrix (Group B & C), making SSC Selection Post Phase 14 a good opportunity for 10th/12th/Graduate candidates seeking Central Government jobs. The online application process has started on 13-04-2026 and will remain active till 04-05-2026, and candidates can download the official notification PDF and apply online through the direct links given below.

SSC Recruitment 2026 Selection Post Phase 14 Notification PDF

The SSC Selection Post Phase 14 Notification PDF 2026 for Matriculation, Higher Secondary and Graduate level posts with Advertisement No. Phase‑XIV/2026/Selection Posts was released by the Staff Selection Commission on 13 April 2026 at ssc.gov.in. The detailed notification provides post category-wise vacancies, region-wise vacancy distribution, eligibility, age limits, application fee, exam pattern and selection process for 2919 Selection Posts in Central, Eastern, Northern, Western and other SSC regions. Candidates should read the official notice and subsequent addendum carefully before filling the SSC Phase 14 online form.

SSC Recruitment 2026 Age Limit

  • Age limits for SSC Selection Post Phase 14 vary post-wise and fall into ranges such as 18–25, 18–27, 18–28, 18–30, 18–35, 20–25, 20–30, 21–27, 21–28, 22–28 and 23–28 years as per the User Department requirements.
Category Age Relaxation
SC / ST 5 years relaxation on upper age limit
OBC (NCL) 3 years relaxation on upper age limit
PwBD (Unreserved) 10 years
PwBD (OBC) 13 years
PwBD (SC/ST) 15 years
Ex‑Servicemen Relaxation as per Government rules (subject to maximum age limit) after deduction of military service from actual age

The exact crucial date for determining age, category-wise relaxations and upper age limits for each post are specified in the SSC Phase‑XIV/2026 notification, and candidates must refer to the post details before applying.

SSC Selection Post Phase 14 Application Fee 2026

  • For General / OBC / EWS candidates: ₹100/- examination fee to be paid online.
  • For Women, SC, ST, PwBD and Ex‑Servicemen candidates eligible for reservation: No fee (Exempted) as per SSC rules.
  • Payment Mode: Online through BHIM UPI, Net Banking, or by using Visa, MasterCard, Maestro, RuPay Credit or Debit Cards within the prescribed time limit.

SSC Selection Post Phase 14 Selection Process 2026

  • Computer Based Examination (CBE): SSC will conduct separate CBTs for Matriculation, 10+2 and Graduation & above level posts consisting of Objective Type Multiple Choice Questions with 100 questions (25 each from General Intelligence, General Awareness, Quantitative Aptitude and English Language).
  • Exam Duration: 60 minutes for general candidates and 80 minutes for eligible candidates using scribes; there is negative marking of 0.50 marks for each wrong answer.
  • Skill Tests: Skill Tests like Typing/Data Entry/Computer Proficiency Tests etc., where prescribed for particular posts, will be of qualifying nature and conducted by the User Departments.
  • Document Verification (DV): Shortlisted candidates will be called by the respective User Departments for Document Verification and final selection as per post-wise and category-wise vacancies.

SSC Selection Post Phase 14 Salary 2026

Pay Component Details
Pay Level Selection Posts are placed in Pay Levels 1 to 8 of the 7th CPC Pay Matrix, varying according to the post and User Department.
Approximate Pay Basic pay as per level plus Dearness Allowance, House Rent Allowance, Transport Allowance and other admissible allowances as per Central Government norms.

SSC Selection Post Phase 14 Documents Required

No supporting documents/certificates are required to be uploaded at the time of online application for SSC Selection Post Phase 14; however, candidates must possess the original documents for verification.

  • Matriculation, 10+2 and Graduation mark sheets and certificates to support educational qualifications.
  • Caste certificate for SC/ST/OBC, EWS certificate, PwBD certificate and Ex‑Servicemen documents where applicable, in prescribed formats.
  • Photo identity proof such as Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Driving Licence, Passport etc. for identity verification at exam and DV stages.

How to Apply for SSC Recruitment 2026 (Selection Post Phase 14)

  • Candidates must apply online only through the SSC website https://ssc.gov.in or the mySSC mobile app within the application window from 13-04-2026 to 04-05-2026 (up to 23:00 Hrs).
  • Register on the SSC portal (One‑time registration) if not already registered, using a valid mobile number and email ID, then log in to apply for Selection Post Phase 14.
  • Fill in all details in the online form such as personal information, category, educational qualification, post preferences and exam centre choices accurately as per original documents.
  • Upload scanned photograph and signature in the prescribed format and size as specified in the notification.
  • Pay the application fee (if applicable) through the available online payment modes before 05-05-2026 (up to 23:00 Hrs).
  • Use the “Application Form Correction Window” between 11-05-2026 and 13-05-2026 to correct mistakes if required and pay the correction charges as applicable.
  • After successful submission and fee payment, download/print the final submitted application form for future reference; candidates will be provisionally admitted to the examination subject to verification at later stages.

Important Instructions for Candidates

  • Before filling the online application, candidates must read the full SSC Phase‑XIV/2026 Selection Posts notice carefully and ensure that they meet all eligibility conditions for the posts they apply for.
  • All particulars filled in the online application must match the original certificates, and any mismatch detected at Document Verification will result in cancellation of candidature.
  • Application fee is non‑refundable and no request for change in category, post, exam centre or any other details will be entertained after final submission.
  • Provisional admission to the exam does not imply final selection; final appointment is subject to verification of eligibility, documents and other conditions by User Departments.
  • Candidates are advised to visit SSC’s official website and the websites of User Departments regularly for updates on exam schedule, admit cards, results and DV schedule; no separate correspondence will generally be made.
  • PwBD, Ex‑Servicemen and other reserved category candidates must possess and produce valid certificates in the prescribed format at the time of Document Verification to avail reservation/relaxation benefits.
  • Any form of canvassing, use of unfair means or providing false information will lead to disqualification and possible legal action; SSC’s decision in all matters relating to recruitment will be final and binding.

The latest Central Bank of India (CBI) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Assistant General Manager (AGM) - Specialist Category posts! Published on 30 April 2026 at www.centralbank.bank.in, this new recruitment drive offers 15 openings for eligible candidates. Applicants within the 33 to 45 years age group and possessing qualifications such as CA/CFA/CMA, MBA/PGDM, B.E./B.Tech, PG degree/diploma, or relevant specialist certification with required experience can apply online from 30 April 2026 to 17 May 2026. Find all details about the CBI AGM Specialist new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

FOLLOW US

Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Central Bank of India, a leading Public Sector Bank with PAN India branch network of more than 4500 branches and total business of more than Rs. 8,12,000 Crores, has officially released a Recruitment Process Notification for selection of 15 Assistant General Manager (AGM) in Specialist Category in Risk Management, Finance & Accounts and Credit (Scale V). The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility criteria, educational qualification, experience, age limit, selection process, examination pattern, application fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification carefully before applying online. The notification PDF is available on the official Central Bank of India website.

Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 15 vacancies have been announced for the post of Assistant General Manager (Scale V) in Specialist Category. These are distributed across SC, ST, OBC, EWS and UR categories as follows. The number of vacancies is provisional and may vary according to actual bank requirement. Reservation for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD (Divyangjan) categories is applicable as per Government of India guidelines.

Sr. No. Post Scale Total
1 Risk Management
i Risk Management V 2
ii Compliance / Risk Management 1
iii Asset Liability Management 1
iv Digital Risk & Analytics 1
2 Finance & Accounts 5
3 Credit Officer 5
Total 15

Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date 31-03-2026. All educational qualifications must be from a university recognized by Govt. of India / approved by Govt. Regulatory Bodies. Any experience of less than 6 months in an organization shall not be considered for determining eligibility, except when such experience is part of current employment. The post-wise educational qualification and experience requirements are as follows:

Post-wise Educational Qualification, Experience and Age Limit

Post Name Essential Educational Qualification Additional/Mandatory Certification Experience
AGM - Risk Management (Scale V) Full-time B.Sc in Statistics/Economics / Bachelor's in Analytics (Statistics, Applied Maths, OR/Data Science) with 55% marks OR Full-time MBA Finance/Banking or PGDBM/IPGD-Risk Management with 55% marks - from AICTE/UGC approved University. Mandatory: FRM (GARP USA) / CFA (CFA Institute USA) / PRM (PRMIA USA). Preferable: Certification in SPSS/SAS, R, Python. Minimum 10 years in Banking (Officer Cadre), out of which minimum 5 years in Risk Management in scheduled commercial banks having more than 2000 branches. Current role must be Chief Manager with min. 1 year OR min. 4 years as Senior Manager.
AGM - Compliance / Risk Management (Scale V) Full-time B.Sc in Statistics/Economics / Bachelor's in Analytics with 55% marks OR Full-time MBA Finance/Banking or PGDBM/IPGD-Risk Management with 55% marks - from AICTE/UGC approved University. Mandatory: FRM (GARP USA) / CFA (CFA Institute USA) / PRM (PRMIA USA). Preferable: Certification in SPSS/SAS, R, Python. Minimum 10 years in Banking (Officer Cadre), out of which minimum 5 years in Risk Management/Compliance Dept. in scheduled commercial banks having more than 2000 branches. Current role must be Chief Manager with min. 1 year OR min. 4 years as Senior Manager.
AGM - Asset Liability Management (Scale V) Full-time BE/B.Tech OR Post Graduate Degree/Diploma in Business Management/Banking/Finance OR Chartered Accountant. Mandatory: FRM (GARP) / PRM (PRMIA USA) / CFA (CFA Institute). Minimum 10 years in Banking (Officer Cadre), out of which minimum 5 years in Risk Management in scheduled commercial banks having more than 2000 branches. Current role must be Chief Manager with min. 1 year OR min. 4 years as Senior Manager. Desirable: ALM/Interest Rate Risk/Liquidity Risk management.
AGM - Digital Risk & Analytics (Scale V) Full-time Bachelor's in Mathematics/Statistics OR B.E./B.Tech in Data Science/IT/CS/Software Engineering. Preferred: MBA/PGDM/PGDBM/PGDBA in Finance OR PG in Statistics/Mathematics/Economics/Econometrics. Preferred: FRM by GARP, RAI by GARP, PRM (PRMIA), CFA, CQF, any Certification in ML/AI/NLP/Web Crawling/Neural Networks. Minimum 10 years in Banking (Officer Cadre), out of which minimum 5 years in Risk Management in scheduled commercial banks having more than 2000 branches. Current role must be Chief Manager with min. 1 year OR min. 4 years as Senior Manager. Desirable: Digital Risk/Vendor Risk/IT Risk/Model Validation.
AGM - Finance & Accounts (Scale V) Mandatory: Full-time Graduation AND must have passed final exam conducted by ICAI and enrolled as member of ICAI (Chartered Accountant). CA membership mandatory. Minimum 10 years in Scheduled Commercial Bank having more than 2000 branches, out of which minimum 7 years at Bank's Head Office/Administrative Office handling Corporate Taxation, GST, TDS, Balance Sheet, IFRS, Ind AS, Regulatory Reporting, Capital Raising. Current role must be Chief Manager with min. 1 year OR min. 4 years as Senior Manager.
AGM - Credit Officer (Scale V) Chartered Accountant / CFA / CMA-ICWA from recognized institute OR Two Years Full-time Post Graduation in Banking/Finance/any credit related field from recognized university/institution/board. As per qualification above. Minimum 10 years post qualification experience as an officer, out of which 7 years in Corporate Credit/Project Finance/Mid & Large Credit in Scheduled Public/Private Sector Banks having more than 2000 branches. Current role must be Chief Manager with min. 1 year OR min. 4 years as Senior Manager.

Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit & Relaxation

Age limit for all posts is Minimum 33 years and Maximum 45 years as on 31-03-2026. Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India guidelines as follows:

AGM - Asset Liability Management (Scale V) Min 33 years - Max 45 years
AGM - Digital Risk & Analytics (Scale V) Min 33 years - Max 45 years

AGM - Credit Officer (Scale V) Min 33 years - Max 45 years

Children/Family members of those who died in 1984 riots 5 years
Ex-Servicemen (with at least 5 years military service) 5 years

Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Application fees / intimation charges are payable online from 30-04-2026 to 17-05-2026. The fee once paid is non-refundable. Bank transaction charges for online payment will have to be borne by the candidate.

Category Amount (inclusive of GST)
SC / ST / PwBD (Divyangjan) / Women Candidates Rs. 175/- (Intimation Charges only)
All Other Candidates (General / OBC / EWS) Rs. 850/- (Application Fee + Intimation Charges)

Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process consists of an Online Examination followed by an Interview. Merely satisfying the eligibility norm does not entitle a candidate to be called for the test or interview. Candidates qualified in the online test will be called for interview to the extent of 4 times the number of vacancies. Final merit will be based on the combined performance in Online Test and Interview in the ratio of 70:30. In case of equal marks, candidates senior in age will be placed higher in the merit list.

Online Examination Structure:

Sr. No. Name of Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration
1 Stream/Category Specific Questions 70 70 Composite time of 60 minutes
2 Banking, Present Economic Scenario & General Awareness 30 30
Total 100  

Qualifying Marks

Category Qualifying Marks - Written Test Qualifying Marks - Interview
General / EWS 50% 50%
SC / ST / OBC / PwBD (Divyangjan) 45% 45%

Selection Weightage

Online Test : Interview Weightage 70 : 30
No. of candidates called for Interview 4 times the number of vacancies (category-wise)
Negative Marking No penalty for wrong answers

How to Apply for Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026

Candidates can apply only online from 30-04-2026 to 17-05-2026 at the link https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/cbiagmapr26/. No other mode of application will be accepted. Before applying, candidates should scan their photograph (200x230 pixels, 20-50 KB), signature (140x60 pixels, 10-20 KB), left thumb impression (240x240 pixels, 20-50 KB), and handwritten declaration (800x400 pixels, 50-100 KB). A valid personal email ID and contact number must be kept active till completion of the recruitment process. Not more than one application should be submitted by any candidate. In case of multiple applications, only the latest valid (completed) application will be retained.

An indemnity bond of INR 3,00,000/- must be executed by the selected candidate to indemnify the Bank in case such candidate leaves the service within 3 years of selection. Candidates must have a minimum CIC (Credit Information Company) score of 650 with no credit default or financial indiscipline as on the date of document verification.

Documents to be Produced at the Time of Interview (Original + Self-attested copies):

  • Original Marks Sheet & Provisional Certificate / Degree Certificate issued from University
  • Experience Certificates as per eligibility requirements
  • Caste / PwBD (Divyangjan) / Caste Validity Certificate (wherever applicable)
  • OBC Certificate with Non-Creamy Layer clause (for OBC candidates)
  • EWS Income and Asset Certificate (for EWS candidates)
  • No Objection Certificate from employer (for candidates serving in Govt./PSU/Nationalized Banks)
  • Colour passport size photograph (same as uploaded in application form)

Apply Online at: https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/cbiagmapr26/

Important Instructions for Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Only one application per candidate - multiple applications will result in forfeiture of fee paid for other registrations.
  • Applications once submitted cannot be withdrawn and fee once paid is neither refunded nor held in reserve.
  • Candidates must ensure eligibility before applying - the Bank reserves the right to reject applications at any stage.
  • Minimum CIC score of 650 is mandatory with no credit default in the credit report.
  • Indemnity bond of INR 3,00,000/- must be executed if selected (valid for 3 years of service).
  • Probation period of 2 years from the date of joining.
  • Initial posting will be at Mumbai and thereafter transferable anywhere in India as per bank's transfer policy.
  • Government/PSU/Nationalized Bank employees must produce NOC from employer at the time of interview.
  • OBC candidates in 'Creamy Layer' will be treated as 'General Category'.
  • No Travelling Allowance is payable to candidates called for interview. However, unemployed SC/ST candidates will be paid 2nd Class Rail/Ordinary Bus fare on production of travel evidence.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.
  • Any resulting dispute shall be subject to the sole jurisdiction of Courts situated at Mumbai.
  • Candidates are advised to regularly visit the Bank's website www.centralbank.bank.in for updates under Career section.
  • All decisions of the Bank regarding eligibility, selection, and other matters are final and binding on all candidates.
