TMB Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

No application fee is required for any post under TMB Recruitment 2026.

Category Application Fee
All Categories Nil (No Fee)

TMB Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for all posts consists of shortlisting of applications followed by a Personal Interview. Shortlisted candidates will be called for personal interview through Direct / Video Conferencing. The mode of interview, date, and time will be communicated to eligible candidates individually. The Bank reserves the right to reject any application at any point of time without assigning any reason. Bank's decision in the matter of eligibility, conduct of interview, and selection will be final.

Stage Process
Stage 1 Scrutiny / Shortlisting of Applications
Stage 2 Personal Interview (Direct / Video Conferencing)

How to Apply for TMB Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through TMB's official career portal at www.ib.tmbonline.bank.in/tmb_careers/. No other mode of submission of application will be accepted. The application portal is active from 29-04-2026 to 19-05-2026. Candidates must register their correct email ID and mobile number while creating User ID - these must be kept active till completion of the recruitment process. On successful registration, an activation link will be sent to the registered email ID and the Registration Number and Password will be sent to the registered mobile number. Candidates should note this Unique Registration Number and Password for future reference.

Candidates are required to upload their photograph (390 x 520 pixels, max 120 KB) and signature (240 x 240 pixels, max 60 KB - signature in Capital letters will NOT be accepted). The following enclosures must be uploaded along with the e-application (max 250 KB each).

Documents to be Uploaded Along with Online Application:

  • Self-attested photo copy of SSLC or Higher Secondary Mark Sheet / Transfer Certificate for age proof
  • Self-attested photo copy of Degree Certificate(s)
  • Experience Certificates and Relieving Letter for previous employments
  • Last Month Salary Slip

Apply Online at: www.ib.tmbonline.bank.in/tmb_careers/

Important Instructions for TMB Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Applications must be submitted online only - no other mode will be accepted.
  • No application fee is required for any post.
  • Candidates must ensure eligibility before applying - ineligible candidates will be disqualified at any stage.
  • Register correct email ID and mobile number while creating User ID - keep them active till recruitment completion.
  • Note your Unique Registration Number and Password - failing which you will not be able to proceed further.
  • Signature in Capital letters will NOT be accepted.
  • No vigilance case or adverse observation from RBI/Statutory Authority shall be pending against any candidate identified for appointment.
  • For Law Officer post - only candidates from Maharashtra (Mumbai) state are eligible.
  • IT vacancies will be posted at Chennai; GNC Head at Ernakulam; VP Accounts & DVP Secretarial Section at Thoothukudi; Law Officer at Maharashtra; remaining posts are PAN India.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.
  • Job role and location are subject to transfer based on requirements.
  • Applications with incorrect/incomplete particulars or without required certificates are liable to be rejected.
  • The Bank reserves the right to reject any application without assigning any reason - Bank's decision is final.
  • No correspondence will be entertained by the Bank regarding the recruitment.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Jammu & Kashmir Bank has published the Apprenticeship Notification 2026-27 under the Apprentices Act, 1961 (NATS). The recruitment drive aims to engage 614 Apprentices for one year in various Branches/Offices across UT of J&K, UT of Ladakh, and Rest of India (ROI). Graduates who passed in or after 01.01.2022 are eligible to apply online. The online registration process is active from 20 April 2026 to 03 May 2026. Candidates can download the official notification PDF and apply through the links below.

FOLLOW US

J&K Bank Apprenticeship Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Jammu & Kashmir Bank has released the detailed advertisement (Ref. No. JKB/HR/Rectt/2026-182) for engagement of Apprentices under National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS). Candidates can download the official detailed advertisement from the J&K Bank website and check district-wise/region-wise vacancies, eligibility, fee and application process before applying online.

  • Download J&K Bank Apprenticeship Notification 2026 PDF 
  • Extended J&K Bank Apprenticeship Notification 2026 PDF

J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria

Educational Qualification Graduation from a recognized University/Institute. Candidates must have passed their Graduation on or after 01.01.2022. The candidate should be proficient in the local/official language of the respective region and should have studied it as a subject in 10th or 12th.

Age Limit (as on 01.01.2026)

  • Minimum Age: 20 years
  • Maximum Age: 28 years Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD etc. as per Government guidelines.

Stipend ₹13,500/- per month (Bank Share ₹9,000 + Govt. Share ₹4,500 via DBT). Conveyance allowance up to ₹1,250/- per month.

J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Application Fee

Category Application Fee
Unreserved (General) ₹700/-
Reserved Category (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD) ₹500/-

J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Selection Process

The selection of apprentices for J&K Bank 2026 will be based entirely on merit obtained in an Online Written Examination of objective type. The examination consists of 100 questions of 100 marks to be completed in 60 minutes (1 hour), divided into two sections: General Awareness & English Comprehension (50 questions, 50 marks, 30 minutes) and Quantitative Aptitude & Reasoning Ability (50 questions, 50 marks, 30 minutes).

There is a penalty for wrong answers -one-fourth (1/4th) of the marks assigned to a question will be deducted for each wrong answer. No penalty applies for unattempted questions. The online examination will be conducted only in English. The merit list will be drawn district/region and category wise, with candidates placed according to their aggregate marks in descending order. In case two or more candidates score equal marks, they will be ranked based on age in descending order (older candidate ranked higher).

A wait list of 50% of notified vacancies will be maintained for one year from the date of result or till the next engagement exercise, whichever is earlier. The Bank reserves the right to conduct any other test or follow any other selection criteria at its discretion.

S. No. Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration
1 General Awareness & English Comprehension 50 50 30 Minutes
2 Quantitative Aptitude & Reasoning Ability 50 50 30 Minutes
Total 100 100 60 Minutes

Additional Benefits for J&K / Ladakh Domicile Apprentices (on Future Recruitment):

  • Relaxation of one year in age for appointment in Banking Associate cadre after successful completion of the Apprenticeship programme (applicable exclusively for domicile of UTs of J&K and Ladakh).
  • An additional 2.5% of maximum marks (i.e., 2.5 marks out of 100) will be added to the score in the online written test, subject to the condition that total marks (including bonus marks) do not exceed maximum marks (applicable exclusively for domicile of UTs of J&K and Ladakh).

J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Application Form

The application form for J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is available exclusively through the online mode on the official J&K Bank website at https://www.jkb.bank.in. No offline or paper-based application form will be accepted under any circumstances. Before filling the online application form, candidates must first complete their registration on the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS) portal at https://nats.education.gov.in and obtain their enrolment number. Candidates must ensure that their Mobile number, Aadhaar, and Bank account are inter-linked and DBT is enabled in their bank account before proceeding with the application.

The online application form requires candidates to fill in personal details, educational qualification details, category details, district/area preference, and upload scanned documents including photograph, signature, left thumb impression, and a handwritten declaration in English. Candidates are advised to verify all details carefully before clicking the final "Complete Registration" button, as no change will be permitted after final submission. After successful payment of the application fee, candidates must take a printout of the e-Receipt and the online application form for future reference.

Eligible candidates wishing to apply for J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 must complete the online application process between 20 April 2026 and 03 May 2026. Applications submitted after the closing date or through any mode other than online will not be considered.

Submit Online Application at: https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/jkbamar26/ 

J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Salary / Stipend

Component Amount Disbursed By
Bank's Share of Stipend ₹9,000/- per month J&K Bank (directly)
Government's Share of Stipend ₹4,500/- per month Government of India via DBT (for candidates below 35 years of age and first-time apprentices only)
Total Monthly Stipend ₹13,500/- per month Combined (Bank + Govt.)
Conveyance Allowance Up to ₹1,250/- per month J&K Bank
Other Allowances / Benefits NIL Not Applicable

How to Apply for J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026

  • Eligible candidates can apply for J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 through online mode only from 20 April 2026 to 03 May 2026. No other mode of application will be accepted.
  • Before applying, candidates must first register on the Apprenticeship Portal at https://nats.education.gov.in and obtain their enrolment number.
  • Candidates must complete 100% of their profile on the Apprenticeship Portal and ensure their Mobile number, Aadhaar, and Bank account are inter-linked with DBT enabled.
  • Thereafter, candidates must apply on the official J&K Bank website at https://www.jkb.bank.in under the Careers tab by clicking on the "Engagement of Apprentices" link.
  • Candidates can apply for only one District/Area; if more than one application is submitted, only the latest application will be considered. Candidates must upload scanned copies of their photograph, signature, left thumb impression, and a handwritten declaration in English as per the specified format and dimensions.
  • After submitting the application and paying the fee, candidates must take a printout of the e-Receipt and online Application Form for future reference. No change of data is permitted after the final submission of the online application.

Documents to Upload at the Time of Online Application:

  • Passport-size colour photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm; 20KB–50KB; JPG/JPEG)
  • Signature in black ink on white paper (10KB–20KB; JPG/JPEG)
  • Left thumb impression on white paper with black or blue ink (20KB–50KB; JPG/JPEG)
  • Handwritten declaration in English on white paper with black ink (50KB–100KB; JPG/JPEG)

J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Examination Centres

The online written examination for J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 will be conducted at designated centres across multiple cities.

The examination will be held online at venues mentioned in the respective call letters. No request for change of centre, venue, date, or session will be entertained under any circumstances. The choice of centre once exercised by the candidate is final.

The Bank reserves the right to cancel any examination centre, add new centres, or allot a different centre to a candidate depending on the response and administrative feasibility.

Candidates will appear for the examination at their own risk and expense. Though the duration of the examination is 1 hour, candidates may be required to remain at the venue for approximately 4 hours to complete formalities such as document verification and login procedures.

Candidates reporting late after the reporting time specified on the call letter will not be permitted to take the examination.

Available Examination Centres
Srinagar, J&K Bangalore, Karnataka Pune, Maharashtra
Jammu, J&K Baramulla, J&K Samba, J&K
Leh, Ladakh Kargil, Ladakh Delhi / Delhi NCR
Mumbai / Navi Mumbai / Thane / MMR, Maharashtra Lucknow, U.P. Chandigarh
Mohali    

Documents Required at the Time of Engagement

Candidates who are shortlisted and called for engagement as apprentices must produce the following documents in original along with a self-attested photocopy at the time of engagement. Non-submission of any of the required documents will make the candidate ineligible for further participation in the engagement process.

Candidates are advised to keep all documents ready well in advance to avoid last-minute delays. All documents must match exactly the details submitted in the online application form. Any discrepancy found in the documents at any stage of the process may lead to disqualification or termination of apprenticeship, if already engaged.

  • Valid system generated printout of online application
  • Proof of Date of Birth (SSLC / Class X Certificate with DOB)
  • Photocopy of photo identity proof bearing the same name as in the online application
  • Photocopy of Aadhaar and PAN Card
  • Mark sheets and certificates of Graduation or equivalent qualification
  • Two recent passport-size photographs
  • Printout of Online Result
  • Permanent Resident / Domicile Certificate
  • Other qualification certificates (if any)
  • Income and Asset Certificate issued by competent authority (for EWS candidates)
  • Category Certificate (if applicable)
  • Caste Certificate issued by competent authority in Government of India prescribed format (for SC/ST/OBC candidates); OBC certificate must contain non-creamy layer clause and must be valid as on the date of notification
  • PCP/Disability Certificate issued by the District Medical Board (for PwBD candidates)
  • Scribe certificate, if used during examination

Union Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date 01-04-2026. Candidates should NOT have undergone Apprenticeship either with Union Bank of India or in any other organization earlier, or be currently pursuing Apprenticeship Training as per the Apprentices Act 1961 as amended. Candidates who had completed apprenticeship training or had job experience for one year or more after completing the educational qualification are also not eligible.

The candidate applying for trainee seats of a particular state should be proficient (reading, writing, speaking and understanding) in any one of the local languages of that state. Candidates must possess the prescribed minimum qualification on the cut-off date; admission to all stages is provisional and subject to eligibility verification.

Educational Qualification

Qualification Details
Graduate Degree Graduate degree in any discipline from any Institute / College / University recognized / approved by Govt. bodies / AICTE / UGC. The result must have been declared on or before 01.04.2026. The date of passing Graduation should not be more than 4 years preceding the cut-off date (i.e. Graduation Marksheet should not be prior to 01.04.2022).
Nationality / Citizenship Must be a Citizen of India or subject of Nepal / Bhutan / eligible person of Indian origin as per prescribed criteria. A certificate of eligibility from Govt. of India is required for non-Indian citizens in eligible categories.
Local Language Proficiency Candidate must be proficient in any one of the local languages of the applied state (reading, writing, speaking and understanding). Evidence via 10th/12th marksheet/certificate or local language proficiency test.

Candidates must be minimum 20 years and maximum 28 years of age as on 01-04-2026. Relaxation in upper age limit is available for reserved categories as specified below. Candidates belonging to OBC category falling in the creamy layer are not entitled to OBC age relaxation and should apply under UR/General category.

Union Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates applying for the post of Apprentice are required to pay a non-refundable examination fee and intimation charges as specified below. The fee must be paid online through the BFSI SSC application portal. The application process is complete only when the examination fee is paid to BFSI SSC through online mode on or before the last date of application. Fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.

3 SC / ST Nil Rs. 200/- Rs. 236/-
4 PWBD Nil Rs. 200/- Rs. 236/-
5 Transgender Nil Rs. 200/- Rs. 236/-

Union Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for Union Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 consists of 2 (two) stages - an Online Test of 100 marks (objective type, 60 minutes) and a Proficiency Test of Local Language. Candidates who qualify the online test with cut-off marks as decided by the Bank will be called for document verification and the local language proficiency test. Final selection will be subject to meeting cut-off marks, satisfactory document verification, proficiency of local language, and submission of a Medically Fit Certificate from a registered General Physician.

Total duration of examination: 60 Minutes. No Negative Marking.

Stage 2 - Proficiency Test of Local Language

The candidate applying for vacancies of a particular state must be proficient in any one of the local languages of that state (reading, writing, speaking and understanding). Selected candidates will have to produce their 10th/12th standard marksheet/certificate evidencing having studied the opted local language. Candidates unable to produce such certificate will be required to give a test of local language.

Union Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Stipend / Benefit

The engaged apprentices are eligible for the following stipend per month for the contract period of one year. No other allowances/benefits are applicable. The stipend will be paid on completion of each month after adjusting unauthorized absence, if any. Labour laws provisions such as PF, ESI, Bonus shall NOT be applicable to apprentices.

Branch / Office Category Stipend (per month)

Urban Rs. 18,000/-
Metro Rs. 20,000/-

How to Apply for Union Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the BFSI SSC portal. Hard copy of application and other documents must not be sent to the Bank's office. Candidates are requested to apply online through https://unionbankofindia.bank.in/recruitment.aspx or https://beep.bfsissc.com/candidate_register/mkt. All candidates must also register on the NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in/) and NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in/student_type.php) before applying and note their Apprentice Registration Code (NAPS) and Enrolment ID (NATS) for future correspondence.

Candidates should avoid submitting multiple applications; if done unavoidably, only the last valid (completed) application will be retained, and fee paid against other applications will stand forfeited. No change of data is allowed after final submission of the online application.

Documents to be Kept Ready Before Applying Online:

  • Aadhaar card front and back side (Format: JPEG, Size: Less than 1 MB each)
  • Valid Personal Email ID and active Mobile Number
  • Passport size photograph (Format: JPEG, Size: Less than 1 MB)
  • Qualifying Marksheet / Degree / Provisional Certificate (Format: PDF, Size: Less than 1 MB)
  • Caste Certificate, if applicable (Format: PDF, Size: Less than 1 MB)
  • PwBD Certificate, if applicable (Format: PDF, Size: Less than 1 MB)
  • PAN Card (Format: JPEG, Size: Less than 1 MB)
  • APAAR ID Card (Format: JPEG, Size: Less than 1 MB)
  • NAPS Apprentice Registration Code and NATS Enrolment Number

Examination fee can be paid by Debit Card / Credit Card / Internet Banking / UPI. On successful payment, an e-receipt will be sent to the registered email ID. Call letters for online examination will be communicated via email from [email protected]. No hard copy of the call letter will be sent by post. For queries: [email protected] or [email protected].

Apply Online at: https://unionbankofindia.bank.in/recruitment.aspx

Important Instructions for Union Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • The engagement of apprentices is not an employment in the Bank; it is on-job training to gain experience. The Bank has no obligation to offer regular employment on completion of the program.
  • Candidates should NOT have undergone or be currently pursuing Apprenticeship in any organization under the Apprentices Act 1961 as amended.
  • Candidates who had job experience of one year or more after completing educational qualification are NOT eligible.
  • No hard copy of application or other documents to be sent to the Bank's office – online submission only.
  • Examination fee once paid is non-refundable and cannot be adjusted against any other exam or selection.
  • Not more than one application to be submitted; in case of multiple applications only the last valid application will be retained.
  • All candidates must produce Aadhaar number before engagement (contract signing).
  • The online examination will be conducted using candidate's own camera-enabled device (desktop / laptop / tablet / smartphone).
  • Candidates are NOT permitted to use calculators during the examination.
  • Government employees must produce NOC at the time of interview if application not routed through Proper Channel.
  • Candidates are advised to visit www.unionbankofindia.bank.in regularly for updates on this recruitment.
  • The Apprentices are prohibited from joining / forming any Union Body.
  • All decisions of the Bank regarding eligibility, selection, and other matters are final and binding. Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

MECON Limited has announced the FTFT (Full Time Fixed Tenure) Recruitment 2026 for 103 posts across various engineering, technical, and administrative disciplines. Published on 23rd March 2026 at meconlimited.co.in, this new recruitment drive offers openings for eligible candidates in disciplines such as Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, Mining, HR, Finance, and more. Applicants within the Age Limit Range and holding the required Educational Qualifications can apply online from 27th April 2026 to 11th May 2026. Find all details about the MECON FTFT Recruitment 2026, including eligibility, application process, and notification PDF here.

FOLLOW US

A total of 60 post codes covering a wide range of engineering, technical, and administrative positions have been advertised. Candidates are advised to download and read the official notification carefully before submitting their online application to ensure they meet all eligibility conditions and are aware of the complete application and interview process.

MECON Limited FTFT Recruitment 2026 - Pay & Other Benefits

In addition to the consolidated monthly pay mentioned against each post, MECON offers a comprehensive benefits package to all contractual employees. Employees deployed at site/project locations receive a Site Allowance of 7.5% of pay, while those at Metro locations receive a Metro Allowance of 2.5% of pay. An annual increment on consolidated pay (rounded to the next ten rupees) is provided subject to company rules. Medical facilities including OPD and IPD treatment are available at Ispat Hospital, Ranchi, for employees posted at Ranchi (covering self, spouse, and 2 dependent children). For other locations, a company-retained doctor provides free consultation. Additionally, reimbursement of up to ₹15,000 per year towards Mediclaim premium (₹4,00,000 on floater basis) is available as per company policy.

Company accommodation (family or bachelor) may be provided based on availability at applicable charges; if not availed, HRA at 5% of consolidated pay is payable. Leave entitlements include Casual Leave (12 days/year), Earned Leave (18 days/year, accumulation up to 45 days), Half Pay Leave (14 days/year with commutation on medical certificate), and Maternity Leave as applicable to regular employees. National, Festival, and Restricted Holidays (4 nos.) are as per regular employee norms. All employees are covered under a Group Personal Accident Policy with a Sum Insured of ₹25 Lakhs.

Benefit Details
Site Allowance 7.5% of Consolidated Pay (for site/project locations)
Metro Allowance 2.5% of Consolidated Pay (for Metro locations)
Annual Increment On consolidated pay as per company rules (rounded to next ₹10)
Medical Facility (Ranchi) OPD, IPD including medicines at Ispat Hospital for self, spouse & 2 dependent children
Medical Facility (Other Locations) Free consultation by company's retainer doctor
Mediclaim Reimbursement Up to ₹15,000/year towards premium for ₹4,00,000 floater Mediclaim policy

Half Pay Leave (HPL) 14 days/year with commutation on production of medical certificate
Maternity Leave As applicable to regular employees
Holidays National Holidays, Festival Holidays & 4 Restricted Holidays as per regular employees
Group Personal Accident Policy ₹25 Lakhs (Sum Insured)

MECON Limited FTFT Recruitment 2026 - Definition of Work Experience

Work experience will be counted only if it is in a full-time job on a regular salary basis and in the relevant area as stipulated against the respective post. Intermittent or freelance experience will not be counted. Entrepreneurial experience is also not eligible. For Management Trainees, Executive Trainees, and Graduate Trainees, only the on-the-job training period served in PSUs will be counted towards experience. Articleship, internship, academic projects, teaching, and research experience will not be considered as post-qualification experience. However, the period of apprenticeship under the Apprenticeship Act will be counted towards the required post-qualification experience. All experience claimed must be substantiated with documentary evidence as specified; self-declarations regarding the nature of work or relevance of experience will not be accepted.

MECON Limited FTFT Recruitment 2026 - Documentary Evidence for Work Experience

Candidates must provide valid work experience certificates for all past employments mentioned in the application form. These certificates must clearly mention the date of joining, date of relieving, nature of duties, and specific areas of experience with relevance to the applied post. In the absence of a certificate from any past employer, the experience from that employer will not be counted, and the candidature may be rejected if minimum experience is not met. For current employment, candidates must submit an Experience Certificate or Offer/Appointment Letter along with Pay Slips for the last 3 months from the date of the advertisement. Identity Card proving continuity of employment may also be submitted. Non-submission of these documents may result in rejection. Aadhaar Card is mandatory and must be uploaded - any mismatch between the Aadhaar number entered online and the uploaded document will lead to summary rejection.

Employment Type Required Documents
Past Employment Experience certificate(s) with date of joining, date of relieving, nature of duties, and relevance to the applied post
Current Employment Experience Certificate OR Offer/Appointment Letter + Last 3 months Pay Slips + Identity Card (to prove continuity)

MECON Limited FTFT Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit & Relaxation

The upper age limit for each post is 50 years for General (UR) and EWS category candidates, as calculated on the cut-off date of 23.03.2026. Age relaxation is provided as per Government of India guidelines. OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) candidates get a relaxation of 3 years, SC/ST candidates get 5 years, and Persons with Disabilities (PwD) get relaxation of 10 years (General), 13 years (OBC-NCL), or 15 years (SC/ST).

Category Age Relaxation
General (UR) / EWS Upper Age Limit as per post (50 years for all posts in this recruitment)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation
SC / ST 5 years relaxation
PwD - General (UR) 10 years relaxation
PwD - OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 13 years relaxation
PwD - SC / ST 15 years relaxation
J&K Domicile (01.01.1980 to 31.12.1989) 5 years relaxation
Ex-Servicemen As per extant Govt. of India guidelines

MECON Limited FTFT Recruitment 2026 - Reservations & Relaxations

Reservations for SC, ST, OBC (Non-Creamy Layer), PwD (Persons with Disability - 40% or above), and EWS candidates are provided as per Government of India guidelines. Reservation for PwD is on a horizontal basis. SC, ST, and OBC (NCL) candidates applying against UR category posts will be considered on General category merit - no age relaxation or fee waiver applies to them in such cases. OBC (NCL) candidates must produce a valid certificate in the format prescribed by the Government of India; the certificate must not be more than 1 year old as on the date of advertisement. EWS candidates must produce an Income and Asset Certificate issued by the competent authority (such as District Magistrate, Revenue Officer not below Tehsildar rank, etc.) in the prescribed format available on MECON's website. The crucial date for the Income and Asset Certificate is the closing date for online applications.

MECON Limited FTFT Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates applying under UR, OBC (Non-Creamy Layer), or EWS category are required to pay a non-refundable application fee of ₹500 (Rupees Five Hundred only). Candidates belonging to SC, ST, PwD, or Ex-Servicemen categories, as well as internal MECON candidates, are fully exempted from paying any fee.

The application fee must be paid online through the payment gateway provided on MECON's official website. No other mode of payment (such as demand draft, postal order, or cash) is accepted. Once the fee is paid, candidates must click the "Verify" button to update the application status to "Submitted." Fee paid will not be refunded under any circumstances, so candidates are strongly advised to verify their eligibility before making payment. Applications submitted without fee or with insufficient fee will be rejected.

Category Application Fee
UR / OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) / EWS ₹500 (Non-Refundable)
SC / ST / PwD / Ex-Servicemen / Internal Candidates NIL (Exempted)

MECON Limited FTFT Recruitment 2026 - Selection Mode

The selection process for MECON FTFT Recruitment 2026 is through Walk-In-Interview. Walk-In-Interviews will be conducted at four locations - Delhi, Ranchi, Kolkata, and Bangalore - and the specific dates and times will be published on the MECON Careers page. Candidates must apply online before appearing for the interview.

On the date of the Walk-In-Interview, document scrutiny will be undertaken; if any claim in the application is found unsubstantiated, the candidate will not be allowed to appear for the interview and no TA will be reimbursed. The final list of selected candidates will be published on MECON's website and communicated via registered email ID only. MECON reserves the right to change the mode of selection at its discretion.

Stage Details
Step 1 Submit Online Application on MECON website between 27.04.2026 and 11.05.2026
Step 2 Check MECON Careers page for Walk-In-Interview schedule (date, time & venue)
Step 3 Appear for Walk-In-Interview at Delhi / Ranchi / Kolkata / Bangalore with all original documents
Step 4 Document Scrutiny on day of Walk-In-Interview
Step 5 Personal Interview
Step 6 Medical Examination (before joining)
Final Result Published on MECON website and communicated via registered email

MECON Limited FTFT Recruitment 2026 - Travelling Allowance (TA) for Interview

Outstation candidates who appear for the Walk-In-Interview are entitled to reimbursement of travelling allowance (TA) for to-and-fro travel by the shortest route from their mailing address (as mentioned in the application) to the interview venue. TA will be paid at III Tier AC (3A) / Chair Car (CC) rail fare rates, or ₹7 per km by road, whichever is less, subject to production of original tickets/receipts and/or boarding passes. Rail fare will be at regular (non-Tatkal/non-premium Tatkal) rates only. Internal MECON candidates will be governed by company service rules.

Candidates should note that scrutiny of applications is conducted on the interview date itself - if any claim is found unsubstantiated, the candidate will not be interviewed and no TA will be paid. TA forms must be submitted along with proof of travel, bank account number, and IFSC code of the bank branch for payment processing.

Grade Travelling Allowance
Contract III Tier A/C (3A) / CC by rail OR ₹7 per km by road - whichever is less, subject to production of ticket/receipt and/or boarding pass. Regular rail fare only (No premium/Tatkal).

MECON Limited FTFT Recruitment 2026 - Place of Posting

The initial place of posting for each post code is as listed in the table below. Post Codes 01 to 23 are for Rampura Agucha Mines / Debari, Rajasthan; Post Codes 24 to 25 for Bangalore, Karnataka; Post Codes 26 to 27 for Bacheli, Chhattisgarh; Post Code 28 for Ranchi, Jharkhand; Post Codes 29 to 35 for Malanjkhand, Madhya Pradesh; and Post Codes 36 to 60 for Burnpur, West Bengal. Candidates should note that the initial place of posting is indicative only - MECON reserves the right to post selected candidates at any project site, location, or office of the company based on organizational requirements. Candidates must be prepared for transfers/postings anywhere in India as per management discretion.

Sl. No. Post Code(s) Place of Posting
1 01 to 23 Rampura Agucha Mines / Debari, Rajasthan
2 24 to 25 Bangalore, Karnataka
3 26 to 27 Bacheli, Chhattisgarh
4 28 Ranchi, Jharkhand
5 29 to 35 Malanjkhand, Madhya Pradesh
6 36 to 60 Burnpur, West Bengal

How to Apply for MECON Limited FTFT Recruitment 2026

Applications for MECON FTFT Recruitment 2026 must be submitted online only through MECON's official website (www.meconlimited.co.in → Careers → Career Opportunities → Apply Online). No offline or postal applications will be accepted. Candidates must apply between 27 April 2026 and 11 May 2026. During the online application process, candidates must upload a recent passport-size colour photograph (jpg/jpeg, max 40 KB), a signature image in blue/black ink (jpg/jpeg, max 40 KB), and self-attested PDF copies of all required documents.

If multiple documents need to be merged, they must be combined into a single PDF not exceeding 1 MB. Aadhaar Card is mandatory and must be uploaded - any mismatch between the Aadhaar number entered online and the uploaded document will result in summary rejection. No modifications are allowed after submission of the online application, so candidates must take utmost care before final submission.

Government/PSU employees must forward their application through Proper Channel or produce an NOC at the time of the interview; failure to do so means they will not be interviewed and no TA will be paid. Hard copies of the online application and all required documents must be brought on the date of the Walk-In-Interview.

Documents to be uploaded online:

  • Recent passport-size colour photograph (jpg/jpeg, max 40 KB)
  • Signature in blue/black ink (jpg/jpeg, max 40 KB)
  • Matriculation/Secondary Board certificate or Municipal Birth Certificate (proof of age)
  • Final/Provisional Graduation and Post-Graduation certificates (as applicable)
  • All semester/year-wise mark sheets or consolidated mark sheet
  • Work experience certificates/documents as per Para 6 of the notification
  • PAN Card and Aadhaar Card
  • Caste certificate - OBC (NCL) / EWS / SC / ST (as applicable; OBC certificate not more than 1 year old)
  • PwD certificate in prescribed Govt. of India format (if applicable)
  • Income & Asset Certificate for EWS candidates as per DoPT OM No.36039/1/2019-Estt (Res) dated 31.01.2019
  • Discharge/Service Certificate (for Ex-Servicemen)
  • J&K Domicile Certificate (if applicable)
  • Meritorious Sportsperson Certificate (if applicable)

Important Instructions for MECON FTFT Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply.
  • All documents must be self-attested; third-party attestation is not required unless specifically mentioned.
  • Original certificates/documents must be produced at the time of the Walk-In-Interview.
  • No modifications are allowed after submission of the online application.
  • Government/PSU employees must produce NOC from present employer at interview.
  • Certificates in languages other than Hindi/English must be accompanied by a certified translation.
  • Candidates must maintain a valid and active email ID and mobile number for at least 1 year - all future communications will be through email only.
  • Canvassing in any form will disqualify the candidature.
  • All disputes are subject to the jurisdiction of Ranchi (Jharkhand) only.
  • MECON's decision in all matters relating to recruitment will be final and binding.

How to Apply for Sarkari Job Online 2026

  1. Find the right Sarkari Job: Use the vacancy table or sector links above on OnlyJobs. Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, or Engineering. Check the last date and eligibility carefully.

  2. Read the official notification: Click on the job listing to open the official notification PDF. Read all eligibility conditions — age limit, educational qualification, experience, category relaxations.

  3. Visit the official recruitment website: Never apply through third-party sites. Use the direct official link provided on OnlyJobs. Look for "Apply Online" or "Register" on the official portal.

  4. Register / Create account: Sign up using your valid email ID and active mobile number. Keep your Aadhaar, bank account details, and email accessible.

  5. Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualifications, category (General/OBC/SC/ST/EWS), address, and other required information accurately.

  6. Upload documents: Scan and upload recent passport-size photograph, signature, 10th certificate (for DOB proof), educational certificates, caste certificate (if applicable). Check file size and format (JPG/PDF as specified).

  7. Pay application fee: Pay via Net Banking, UPI, Debit/Credit Card as applicable. Some categories (SC/ST/PwD/Women) may be exempted from fee. Keep the payment receipt.

  8. Submit and save confirmation: Review all details before final submission. After submitting, download the application printout / PDF confirmation with your registration number. Store it safely for admit card, exam, and future reference.

Why Sarkari Job? Benefits of Government Employment in India

Job Security
Permanent employment — cannot be terminated without due process. Lifelong career stability.
7th Pay Commission Salary
Fixed salary + DA, HRA, TA allowances. DA revised every 6 months. Regular pay revisions.
Medical Benefits
Free or subsidised medical treatment for employee and family under CGHS / state health schemes.
Housing Benefits
Government quarters (where available) or HRA (8–27% of basic pay). No private rent worries.
Pension (NPS / OPS)
Post-retirement monthly pension under NPS or OPS (state-specific). Financial security after service.
Work-Life Balance
Fixed working hours. Paid casual, medical, earned leave. National and state holidays. No overtime culture.
Career Growth
Regular promotions through DPC, ACR-based upgrades, MACP benefits (3 financial upgrades guaranteed).
Children's Education Allowance
Reimbursement of children's school fees (₹2,812/month per child up to 2 children, revised periodically).

Frequently Asked Questions – Sarkari Job 2026

What is Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी)?

Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी) refers to government employment in India — jobs offered by central government departments, state governments, public sector undertakings (PSUs), banks, railways, defence forces, and other government-funded organisations. These jobs offer permanent employment, fixed salary as per 7th Pay Commission, pension, medical benefits, and unmatched job security.

How to find Sarkari Job vacancy 2026 online?

To find online Sarkari Job vacancies in 2026: (1) Visit OnlyJobs daily for updated notifications. (2) Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, PG. (3) Filter by sector — Railway, Banking, SSC, Defence, State PSC, Teaching. (4) Check last dates carefully. (5) Apply through the official department website using the direct link provided. Enable push notifications on OnlyJobs for instant alerts.

What Sarkari Job can I get after 10th pass?

After 10th pass, you can apply for: Railway Group D (RRB), Indian Army Soldier posts, Postal Mail Guard, SSC MTS / Havaldar, Constable GD, Anganwadi Worker/Helper, ITI Apprentice in PSUs (IOCL, HPCL, NTPC), MCL / Coal India posts, and various state government peon/chowkidar posts. Thousands of 10th pass Sarkari Job vacancies open every year.

What is the salary in Sarkari Job 2026?

Sarkari Job salary in 2026 follows the 7th Pay Commission: Group D / MTS: ₹22,000–₹26,000/month. Clerk / Group C: ₹32,000–₹48,000/month. Officer Grade (SSC CGL, Bank PO): ₹47,000–₹85,000/month. IAS/IPS (UPSC): ₹56,100–₹1,44,200 basic pay + DA, HRA, TA, medical and other allowances. State government salaries vary by state pay commission.

Which Bharat Sarkari Job has the most vacancies in 2026?

The largest Bharat Sarkari Job recruitments in 2026 include: Indian Army Agniveer (40,000+ posts), RRB Group D (22,000+ posts), SSC CGL (14,582 posts), SSC MTS (10,000+ posts), UP Police Constable (multiple), Bihar Police (multiple), IBPS Clerk (8,000+ posts), and various state PSC recruitments. Total active sarkari vacancies in India exceed 52,920 as of March 2026.

What is the best website to find Sarkari Job in India?

OnlyJobs is India's most trusted portal for Sarkari Job notifications since 2011, serving over 10 million users. Official direct sources include: ssc.nic.in (SSC), indianrailways.gov.in (Railways), upsc.gov.in (UPSC), ibps.in (Banking), and respective state PSC websites. OnlyJobs aggregates all official notifications in one place with direct apply links.

How to apply for Sarkari Job online in 2026?

To apply for Sarkari Job online: (1) Read official notification PDF. (2) Check eligibility. (3) Visit official recruitment website. (4) Register with email/mobile. (5) Fill application form. (6) Upload photo, signature, and certificates. (7) Pay fee via Net Banking/UPI/Debit card. (8) Submit and lication confirmation. Save the registration number for admit card and future reference.

What are the benefits of Sarkari Job in India?

Key benefits: (1) Lifelong job security. (2) 7th Pay Commission salary + DA revised biannually. (3) HRA (8–27% of basic). (4) Medical benefits (CGHS). (5) Pension under NPS/OPS. (6) Paid leave — casual, earned, medical. (7) Gratuity after 5 years. (8) Children's education allowance. (9) Fixed working hours and work-life balance. (10) Prestige and social recognition in Indian society.

What is the age limit for Sarkari Job?

Age limits vary by post: Group D (18–25 years), SSC CGL (18–32 years), Bank PO (20–30 years), UPSC Civil Services (21–32 years), Defence Agniveer (17.5–23 years). Age relaxations: SC/ST — 5 years, OBC — 3 years, PwD — 10 years, Ex-Servicemen — 3 years (general), Government servants — up to 5 years. Always verify in the official notification for each post.

What is Bharat Sarkari Job?

Bharat Sarkari Job means government job opportunities across the entire country of India (Bharat) — spanning central and state government vacancies across all 28 states and 8 union territories. The term emphasises pan-India coverage. OnlyJobs covers all Bharat Sarkari Job notifications from UPSC, SSC, Railways, Banking, Defence, state PSCs, and all other government departments.

About OnlyJobs 2026

OnlyJobs (Government Jobs) are highly sought-after employment opportunities in India. These positions offer job security, good salary packages, allowances, and retirement benefits. Our platform provides comprehensive information about all types of government vacancies including:

  • Central Government Jobs: UPSC, SSC, Railway, Banking, Defence, PSUs
  • State Government Jobs: State PSCs, Police, Teachers, Healthcare workers
  • Qualification-based Jobs: 10th Pass, 12th Pass, Graduate, Post Graduate, Engineering
  • Category-wise Jobs: General, OBC, SC, ST, EWS, Ex-Servicemen, PwD

Frequently Asked Questions - OnlyJobs 2026

Q1: What is the eligibility for OnlyJobs?

Eligibility varies by position. Generally, candidates need to be Indian citizens aged 18-35 years (with age relaxation for reserved categories). Educational qualifications range from 10th pass to post-graduation depending on the job.

Q2: How to prepare for Government Job exams?

Preparation includes understanding the exam pattern, studying from standard books, practicing previous year papers, taking mock tests, and staying updated with current affairs. Focus on subjects like General Knowledge, Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, and English.

Q3: Which are the best Government Jobs in 2026?

Top government jobs include IAS/IPS (UPSC), Bank PO, SSC CGL, Railway Group A & B, Defence Services, PSU Jobs, State PCS, and Teaching positions. These offer excellent career growth, job security, and benefits.

Q4: What is the application process for OnlyJobs?

The process typically involves: 1) Online registration on the official website, 2) Filling application form, 3) Uploading documents (photo, signature, certificates), 4) Payment of application fee, 5) Downloading admit card, 6) Appearing for examination, 7) Document verification if selected.

Q5: How often are new OnlyJobs notifications released?

New government job notifications are released daily. Major recruitments like UPSC, SSC, IBPS have annual calendars, while state governments and PSUs release notifications throughout the year. We update our portal daily with 641+ active job listings.

WCD Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Apply Online wcd.nic.in

WCD Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – Apply online for the latest Anganwadi Worker, Anganwadi Helper and Supervisor vacancies through the official WCD/ICDS portals such as wcd.nic.in and state ICDS portals. Candidates can check state-wise and district-wise Anganwadi vacancy lists, eligibility, qualifications, age limit, salary, and online application dates, and download the Anganwadi Application Form 2026. Start your career in Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) and stay updated with the latest Anganwadi job notifications from all major states including Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Telangana, Uttar Pradesh, Andhra Pradesh, Maharashtra, Bihar and more.

Anganwadi Worker Salary / Honorarium 2026

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers receive a monthly honorarium (not a salary), which includes a Central Government component and an additional State Government component. The table below shows the total honorarium per month for key states in 2026.

Note: Honorarium figures are approximate and may vary. Refer to official state notification for exact figures. Additional benefits include ESI, provident fund (where applicable), and national health insurance.

Selection Process for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026

There is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Helper posts. Selection is merit-based on academic qualifications. For Supervisor and CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted.

How to Apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Online

  • Visit the official portal: Go to your state's official WCD / ICDS website or the direct link provided in the vacancy table above. For central information, visit wcd.nic.in.
  • Find the recruitment notification: Click on the "Recruitment" or "Anganwadi Recruitment 2026" section.
  • Download and read the notification: Download and read the official notification PDF carefully before applying.
  • Register / Login: Create a new account using your mobile number and Aadhaar. If already registered, log in with your credentials.
  • Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualification, address, and select your preferred district/ward. Ensure you apply only for a ward where you are a permanent resident.
  • Upload documents: Upload scanned copies of your passport-size photograph, signature, 10th/12th marksheet, Aadhaar card, and any caste/domicile certificate if required.
  • Review and submit: Check all entries carefully before final submission. Download and print the application confirmation / acknowledgement slip for your records.

Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – FAQ's

How to apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 online?

Visit your state's official WCD / ICDS portal (link provided in table above). Register with mobile & Aadhaar, fill the application form, upload documents (photo, 10th certificate, Aadhaar, domicile), and submit before the last date. Print the application acknowledgement slip after submission.

What is the eligibility for Anganwadi Worker 2026?

Candidates must have passed 10th or 12th standard from a recognized board. Age limit is 18–35 years (varies by state). Must be a permanent resident of the ward/village for which vacancy exists. Only female candidates are eligible for Worker and Helper posts.

What is the salary of Anganwadi Worker in 2026?

Anganwadi Worker receives a monthly honorarium of ₹10,000–₹20,000 depending on the state (Karnataka and TN are highest at ~₹14,000). Anganwadi Helper gets ₹5,000–₹7,000/month. The Central Government pays ₹4,500/month as base honorarium, and states add their own component on top.

Is there a written exam for Anganwadi jobs?

No, there is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Anganwadi Helper posts. Selection is purely merit-based on academic qualification marks (10th / 12th percentage). For Supervisor / CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted depending on the state.

What is the age limit for Anganwadi Helper?

Age limit is 18 to 35 years as of 1st January 2026 in most states. SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (maximum age 40). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (maximum age 38). PwD candidates get additional relaxation as per state rules.

What documents are required for Anganwadi job application?

Required documents: Aadhaar card, 10th/12th marksheet and certificate, domicile/residence certificate, caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC), passport-size photograph, scanned signature, and EWS/PwD certificate if applicable. Graduation certificate is needed for Supervisor post applications.

What is ICDS recruitment 2026?

ICDS stands for Integrated Child Development Services - the government programme under which Anganwadi centres operate nationwide. ICDS recruitment 2026 refers to hiring of Anganwadi Workers, Helpers, Supervisors, and CDPOs by state WCD departments under the Ministry of Women & Child Development (wcd.nic.in).

Is Anganwadi Worker a permanent government job?

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers are honorary workers, not regular government employees. They receive a fixed monthly honorarium (not salary) and are entitled to benefits like ESI, health insurance, and maternity benefits. Supervisors and CDPOs are regular government employees in most states and receive full pay & grade pay.

Can I apply for Anganwadi job from a different district?

No. Anganwadi Workers and Helpers must be permanent residents of the same ward/village/district where the vacancy exists. A domicile or residence certificate is mandatory. Applications from outside the specified locality are rejected. For Supervisor/CDPO posts, the same district residency rule generally applies.

Forest Department Posts List

Forest departments work with a clear hierarchy from senior Indian Forest Service officers to field-level staff like Forest Guard and Forest Watcher. Understanding these posts helps candidates know growth opportunities and career progression in forest services.

  • Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (PCCF) / Head of Forest Force
  • Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (APCCF) / Additional Chief Conservator of Forests
  • Chief Conservator of Forests (CCF)
  • Conservator of Forests (CF)
  • Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF) / Divisional Forest Officer (DFO)
  • Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF)
  • Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Range Forest Officer (RFO)
  • Deputy Forest Ranger / Forest Inspector
  • Section Forest Officer / Forester / Van Daroga
  • Beat Forest Officer / Beat Guard / Forester Grade I
  • Forest Guard / Van Rakshak
  • Forest Watcher / Forest Worker / Plantation Worker
  • Forest Ranger (in states where used separately)
  • Field Guard

Most forest department recruitment notifications on this page focus on Forest Guard, Forester, Forest Range Officer, Field Guard and similar Group B, C and D posts.

Eligibility for Forest Department Jobs

Eligibility for forest department jobs depends on the post, state and recruitment authority. Below is a general idea of qualification and age for popular posts like Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer. Candidates should always check the official notification for detailed eligibility criteria.

Education Qualification for Forest Guard, Forester, Ranger

  • Forest Guard / Field Guard / Forest Watcher / Beat Guard: Usually 10th pass or 12th pass from a recognized board, knowledge of local language, and basic physical standards as per state rules.
  • Forester / Section Forest Officer / Deputy Forest Ranger: Generally 12th pass or graduate in any stream or science-related subjects, with physical standards and sometimes driving or technical skills depending on the post.
  • Forest Range Officer (RFO) / Forest Ranger / ACF: Normally graduate degree in Science, Agriculture, Forestry, Environmental Science, Engineering or related disciplines, with higher physical standards and sometimes a competitive exam similar to state PCS level.
  • IFS / Indian Forest Service and senior posts (DCF, CF, CCF, PCCF): Filled through UPSC Indian Forest Service Examination and promotions, requiring a graduate degree and all-India competitive selection.

Age Limit & Relaxation

  • Forest Guard / Forester / Field Guard: Minimum age is usually 18 years and maximum age is around 27–30 years, with age relaxation for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, Ex-servicemen and other categories as per government rules.
  • Forest Range Officer / ACF and higher competitive posts: Age limit often ranges from 21 to 32 years (varies by recruitment), with relaxations for reserved categories.
  • Exact age limit, cut-off date and relaxation details always depend on the particular notification, so candidates should verify from the official advertisement before applying.

Forest Department Jobs Salary Overview

Forest department salary depends on the post, pay level, state and allowances. Senior officers under Indian Forest Service receive higher pay levels, while Forest Guard, Forester and similar posts fall under state government pay matrices.

Post Pay Level Basic Pay Gross Salary (Approx) Category
Forest Guard / Van Rakshak / Vana Palaka Level 3 ₹21,700 ₹28,000 – ₹35,000 Group C
Forester / Forest Sub-Inspector Level 6 ₹35,400 ₹45,000 – ₹56,000 Group C
Forest Beat Officer / Beat Guard / Junior Ranger Level 4 ₹25,500 ₹33,000 – ₹42,000 Group C
Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Ranger Level 10 ₹56,100 ₹70,000 – ₹90,000 Group B
Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF) Level 10 ₹56,100 ₹75,000 – ₹95,000 Group B

Selection Process & Exam Pattern

The selection process for Forest Department Jobs 2026 typically includes written examinations, physical tests and document verification. Some posts also conduct medical examination and walking tests due to the demanding nature of field duties.

Written Exam, PET/PST & Document Verification

  • Written Exam: Most Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer recruitments start with a written exam based on objective-type questions (MCQs).
  • PET / PST (Physical Efficiency Test / Physical Standard Test): Candidates who qualify written exam are called for running, walking test, height, chest measurement and other physical parameters as per post and gender.
  • Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates must produce original certificates of age, qualification, caste, residence, sports/NCC and other documents mentioned in the notification.
  • Medical Examination: Many forest posts require medical fitness to ensure candidates can perform field duties in forests, wildlife areas and difficult terrain.

Syllabus Highlights for Forest Guard & Ranger

Typical syllabus for written exam may include:

  • General Knowledge & Current Affairs (national, state-level, environment and forest-related topics)
  • General Hindi / English or regional language (grammar, comprehension, vocabulary)
  • General Mathematics / Quantitative Aptitude (up to 10th or 12th level)
  • General Science and Environmental Studies (forest, wildlife, biodiversity, conservation, climate, basic biology)
  • Reasoning Ability (logical and analytical reasoning)

Exact subject-wise marks, number of questions, negative marking and exam duration will be given in the respective forest department notification.

How to Apply for Forest Department Jobs 2026

Most Forest Department Jobs 2026 applications are accepted either online through official websites or offline via prescribed application forms. Candidates should carefully read the official notification before filling the form.

Online Application Process

  1. Visit the official website of the recruiting body (State Forest Department, State PSC, Staff Selection Board or other government portal).
  2. Find the latest link for Forest Guard / Forester / Forest Range Officer / other forest posts and open the detailed advertisement.
  3. Complete the registration process with basic details like name, mobile number, email ID and verify using OTP if required.
  4. Fill the online application form with personal, educational, address and category information exactly as per documents.
  5. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature and other certificates in the prescribed format and size.
  6. Pay the application fee online through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card or UPI as per your convenience.
  7. Finally, submit the form and take a printout or PDF of the submitted application and payment receipt for future reference.

Documents Required & Application Fee

  • Recent passport size photograph and signature (scanned)
  • Class 10th and 12th mark sheets and certificates
  • Degree / Diploma certificates for graduate-level posts
  • Caste / Category certificate (SC, ST, OBC, EWS, etc.)
  • Domicile / Residence certificate if required by the state
  • NCC, Sports, Ex-serviceman and other quota certificates if applicable
  • Valid ID proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN, Voter ID, Driving Licence, Passport, etc.)

Why Choose Forest Department Jobs?

Forest department jobs are popular among candidates who love nature, wildlife and field work. These jobs offer job security, government benefits and a chance to work in forest conservation, wildlife protection, eco-tourism and community development.

  • Stable government job with regular salary, pension/NPS and allowances
  • Opportunity to work in forests, wildlife sanctuaries, national parks and protected areas
  • Contribution to environment protection, afforestation and biodiversity conservation
  • Good promotion avenues from Forest Guard to Forester, Forest Range Officer and higher posts in many states.

Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has released the official Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 notification on 23 April 2026 at its official website https://ssc.gov.in. A total of 84 tentative vacancies have been announced for the posts of Junior Hindi Translator, Junior Translation Officer, Junior Translator, Senior Hindi Translator and Senior Translation Officer under various Ministries/Departments/Organizations of the Government of India. Eligible candidates aged between 18 to 30 years with a Master's degree in Hindi or English along with requisite translation diploma or experience can apply online from 23.04.2026 to 14.05.2026. The official notification PDF and application details are available below.

FOLLOW US

SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 Notification PDF

Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has released the official advertisement (F. No. HQ-C11017/1/2026-C-1) for the Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 for direct recruitment to Group 'B' Non-Gazetted posts on a competitive computer-based examination basis. Candidates can download the official notification and check post-wise vacancies, eligibility, fee, exam scheme and application process before applying online.

  • Download SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 Notification PDF (F. No. HQ-C11017/1/2026-C-1)

SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 Overview

Organization Name Staff Selection Commission (SSC), Government of India
Advertisement No. F. No. HQ-C11017/1/2026-C-1
Post Names Junior Hindi Translator (JHT), Junior Translation Officer (JTO), Junior Translator (JT), Senior Hindi Translator (SHT), Senior Translation Officer (STO)
Total Vacancies 84 (Tentative)
Post Category Group 'B' Non-Gazetted
Qualification Master's degree in Hindi/English with translation diploma or experience
Application Mode Online only
Last Date to Apply 14.05.2026 (23:00 hours)
Official Website https://ssc.gov.in

SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Notification Date 23.04.2026
Start Date for Online Application 23.04.2026
Last Date for Online Application 14.05.2026 (23:00 hours)
Last Date for Fee Payment 15.05.2026 (23:00 hours)
Application Form Correction Window 19.05.2026 to 20.05.2026 (23:00 hours)
Paper-I Computer Based Examination August - September 2026

SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 Vacancy

A total of 84 tentative vacancies have been announced across four post codes under the SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026. The vacancies are distributed across various Ministries, Departments, and Organizations of the Government of India including the Central Secretariat Official Language Service (CSOLS) and Armed Forces Headquarters (AFHQ). 

Post-wise Vacancy Details

Code Post Name Pay Scale
A Junior Translation Officer (JTO) - Central Secretariat Official Language Service (CSOLS) Level-6 (₹35,400-1,12,400)
B Junior Translation Officer (JTO) - Armed Forces Headquarters (AFHQ) Level-6 (₹35,400-1,12,400)
C Junior Hindi Translator (JHT) / Junior Translation Officer (JTO) / Junior Translator (JT) - Various Central Government Ministries/Departments/Organizations Level-6 (₹35,400-1,12,400)
D Senior Hindi Translator (SHT) / Senior Translator (ST) / Senior Translation Officer (STO) - Various Central Government Ministries/Departments/Organizations Level-7 (₹44,900-1,42,400)

SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 Eligibility Criteria

  • Nationality: Citizen of India or eligible subjects of Nepal/Bhutan or persons of Indian origin from specified countries with a Government of India eligibility certificate.

Educational Qualification

Post Code Educational Qualification
A, B, C (Junior Level) Master's degree of a recognized University in Hindi with English as compulsory/elective subject at degree level; OR Master's degree in English with Hindi as compulsory/elective at degree level; OR Master's degree in any other subject with Hindi/English as medium or compulsory subject at degree level.
AND
Recognized Diploma/Certificate in translation from Hindi to English & vice versa OR 2 years' experience of translation work from Hindi to English and vice versa in Central/State Government Office including PSUs.
D (Senior Level) Same Master's degree as above.
AND
Recognized Diploma/Certificate in translation from Hindi to English & vice versa OR 3 years' experience of translation work from Hindi to English and vice versa in Central/State Government Office including PSUs.

SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 Age Limit

The age limit for SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 is between 18 and 30 years, calculated as on the crucial date of 01.08.2026. Candidates must have been born not before 02.08.1996 and not later than 01.08.2008 to be eligible. Age relaxation is applicable to candidates belonging to reserved categories as per Government of India rules.

Category Age Limit / Relaxation
General (UR / EWS) 18 - 30 years (as on 01.08.2026)
SC / ST 5 years relaxation (max 35 years)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation (max 33 years)
PwBD (UR) 10 years relaxation
PwBD (OBC) 13 years relaxation
PwBD (SC/ST) 15 years relaxation
Ex-Servicemen (ESM) 3 years after deduction of military service from actual age as on 01.08.2026

SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 Application Fee

The application fee for SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 is ₹100 for General (UR), OBC, and EWS male candidates. Women candidates and candidates belonging to Scheduled Castes (SC), Scheduled Tribes (ST), Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD), and Ex-Servicemen eligible for reservation are fully exempted from payment of fee.

Fee can be paid online only through BHIM UPI, Net Banking, or by using Visa, MasterCard, Maestro, or RuPay Debit Card. The fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances and will not be adjusted against any other examination. Candidates must ensure their fee payment is complete - if the fee is not received by SSC, the application status will show as "Incomplete" and will be summarily rejected.

General / OBC / EWS (Male) ₹100/-
Women / SC / ST / PwBD / Ex-Servicemen NIL (Exempted)

SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 Scheme of Examination

The SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 will consist of two papers. Paper-I is a Computer Based Examination with objective-type multiple choice questions, divided into two sections - General Hindi and General English - each carrying 100 marks with a sectional timer of 1 hour. There is a negative marking of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer in Paper-I.

Based on performance in Paper-I, candidates will be shortlisted category-wise to appear in Paper-II, which is a descriptive paper testing translation and essay writing skills in both Hindi and English, carrying 200 marks. Marks scored in Paper-I will be normalized if the exam is held in multiple shifts. Tentative Answer Keys will be uploaded after the exam, and candidates can submit online representations within the given time on payment of ₹50 per question (non-refundable).

Paper Mode Subject Questions / Marks Duration
Paper-I (Objective) Computer Based Part-I: General Hindi
Part-II: General English		 100/100
100/100		 2 Hours (Sectional timer: 1 hr each part)
{2 hrs 40 min for scribe-eligible candidates}
Paper-II (Descriptive) Pen & Paper Translation (Hindi ↔ English) & Essay 200 Marks 2 Hours
{2 hrs 40 min for scribe-eligible candidates}

SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 Selection Process

The selection process for SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 consists of two stages. In the first stage, Paper-I (Computer Based Examination) is used to shortlist candidates category-wise for Paper-II. Minimum qualifying marks in Paper-I are 30% for UR, 25% for OBC/EWS, and 20% for all other categories. In the second stage, Paper-II (Descriptive) is conducted for shortlisted candidates.

For the final merit list, 50% weightage is given to Paper-I scores and 100% weightage to Paper-II marks. Final selection and allocation of Ministry/Department/Organization is based on merit-cum-preference of posts/departments exercised by candidates through an Online Option Form. In case of a tie, it is resolved by total marks in Paper-II, then date of birth (older preferred), and finally alphabetical order of names. Selected candidates will be on probation for 2 years and all posts carry All India Service Liability (AISL).

Stage Details
1. Paper-I (CBE) Objective type; used for shortlisting category-wise candidates for Paper-II. Minimum qualifying marks: UR 30%, OBC/EWS 25%, Others 20%
2. Paper-II (Descriptive) Translation passages (Hindi ↔ English) and Essay; 200 marks; qualifying based on Paper-I merit
3. Final Merit 50% weightage of Paper-I + 100% weightage of Paper-II marks
4. Post Preference & Allocation Online Option Form to be submitted before FRTA; merit-cum-preference based allocation
5. Document Verification Conducted by User Departments/Organizations after declaration of final result

Tie-Breaking Order

1st Higher total marks in Paper-II
2nd Date of Birth - older candidate preferred
3rd Alphabetical order of names of candidates

How to Apply for SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026

The application process for SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 is entirely online at https://ssc.gov.in. Candidates who have not yet completed their One-Time Registration (OTR) on the new SSC website must do so first, as the earlier OTR from the old website (ssc.nic.in) is not valid for the new portal. Aadhaar-based biometric authentication is available and recommended - candidates who opt for it will not face rejection on grounds of photograph or signature quality.

The application module is designed to capture a live photograph of the candidate through webcam or mobile device; pre-existing photographs must not be submitted. Candidates must also upload their scanned signature in JPEG/JPG format (10-20 KB). Applications with blurred or miniature photographs or signatures will be summarily rejected unless Aadhaar authentication has been opted for. Only one online application per candidate is permitted during the normal application period.

  • Visit the official website https://ssc.gov.in and complete One-Time Registration (OTR).
  • Login using Registration Number and Password.
  • Click on "Apply" under "Combined Hindi Translators Examination, 2026" in the Live Exam tab.
  • Fill in educational details, additional information (ESM/age relaxation), and examination centre preferences (up to 3 centres).
  • Capture live photograph through webcam/mobile as per instructions (no cap, mask, or spectacles).
  • Upload scanned signature in JPEG/JPG format (10-20 KB), dimensions ~6.0 cm (W) × 2.0 cm (H).
  • PwBD/PwD candidates must upload Disability Certificate as per Annexure-I/IA/X/XI, whichever is applicable.
  • Preview the application carefully, complete the declaration, and submit before 14.05.2026 (23:00 hours).
  • Pay the application fee (if applicable) online through BHIM UPI, Net Banking, or Debit Card on or before 15.05.2026 (23:00 hours).
  • Take a printout of the submitted application for future reference.

SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 Application Form

Eligible candidates who wish to apply for the SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 can do so by submitting their application form online only. The application process is entirely online, and candidates must ensure they complete all steps including OTR, form filling, photograph/signature upload, and fee payment before the respective closing dates.

The application form is accessible through the official SSC website after completing One-Time Registration. Candidates are strongly advised to fill the application carefully as only one application is accepted per candidate - multiple applications will lead to cancellation of candidature. Details such as name, father's name, mother's name, and date of birth must be filled exactly as they appear in the Matriculation Certificate. After final submission, modifications are only permitted during the correction window (19.05.2026 to 20.05.2026) on payment of correction charges.

Apply Online at: https://ssc.gov.in

Important Instructions for Candidates

Candidates applying for SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 must read the official notification carefully in its entirety before filling and submitting the application form. The English version of the notice will prevail in case of any dispute. Candidates are advised to submit their applications well before the closing date to avoid last-minute server load issues on the SSC website. All candidatures are provisional - the Commission does not undertake detailed scrutiny at the time of the written examination, and eligibility will be verified only at the Document Verification stage by the User Departments. Any suppression or misrepresentation of information in the application can lead to cancellation of candidature at any stage, including after selection. Canvassing in any form will result in immediate disqualification. The decision of the Commission in all matters including eligibility, selection, and allocation of posts is final and binding.

  • Read the official notice carefully before applying; English version prevails in case of dispute.
  • Only one online application is permitted per candidate; multiple applications will be rejected.
  • Name, DOB, father's name, and mother's name must match the Matriculation Certificate exactly.
  • Candidates must keep a printout of their application for at least 3 years for future reference.
  • Post preference must be submitted online before FRTA declaration; options once submitted are final.
  • All posts carry All India Service Liability (AISL) - candidates may be posted anywhere in India.
  • Regularly check https://ssc.gov.in for updates on exam dates, vacancies, admit cards, and results.

Eligibility Criterion Details

NTPC Assistant Executive (Operation) Application Fee 2026

Candidates belonging to the General/EWS/OBC category are required to pay a non-refundable application fee of ₹500/-. Candidates belonging to SC/ST/PwBD category and female candidates are exempted from payment of the application fee. Fees once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances. Candidates are therefore requested to verify their eligibility criteria thoroughly before paying the application fee.

Payment can be made through online mode via Net Banking / Debit Card / Credit Card on the online application portal. 

Category Application Fee
General / EWS / OBC ₹500/- (Non-refundable)
SC / ST / PwBD / Female Candidates NIL (Exempted)

NTPC Assistant Executive (Operation) Salary / Remuneration 2026

Selected candidates engaged as Assistant Executive (Operation) on Fixed Term basis at NTPC will be entitled to a Fixed Monthly Consolidated Amount of ₹55,000/-. In addition to the consolidated pay, candidates will also receive HRA (House Rent Allowance) or Company Accommodation, Night Shift Allowance (if rostered in night shift), and medical facility for self, spouse, two children, and dependent parents.

No other allowances or perks beyond those mentioned will be admissible to fixed-term employees. The remuneration package is structured to offer competitive compensation while the candidate serves on the fixed-term contractual basis at any of the NTPC Stations, Projects, JVs, or Subsidiaries.

Component Details
Fixed Monthly Consolidated Amount ₹55,000/- per month
HRA Applicable (or Company Accommodation in lieu)
Night Shift Allowance Applicable (if rostered in night shift)
Medical Facility For self, spouse, two children, and dependent parents

NTPC Assistant Executive (Operation) Selection Process 2026

The selection process for NTPC Assistant Executive (Operation) Fixed Term Recruitment 2026 may comprise multiple stages at the discretion of NTPC management. In-order to restrict the number of candidates for further selection process, the management reserves the right to conduct multi-stage selection which may include shortlisting/screening based on qualification/percentage of marks, number of years of experience, written/computer-based test, personal interview, or a combination of the above, subject to meeting the minimum eligibility standards/criteria as per requirement.

Candidates who are shortlisted will be called for further stages. The candidate must undergo a medical examination at any of the NTPC hospitals, and the decision will be final and binding. No relaxation in prescribed health standards is allowed. Detailed medical norms are available on the official NTPC website. Candidates are advised to go through the medical norms carefully before applying.
